master-ball.name: Master Ball
master-ball.description: The best BALL that catches a POKéMON without fail.
master-ball.effect: Used in battle : Catches a wild Pokémon without fail. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

ultra-ball.name: Ultra Ball
ultra-ball.description: A better BALL with a higher catch rate than a GREAT BALL.
ultra-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 2×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

great-ball.name: Great Ball
great-ball.description: A good BALL with a higher catch rate than a POKé BALL.
great-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1.5×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

poke-ball.name: Poké Ball
poke-ball.description: A tool used for catching wild POKéMON.
poke-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

safari-ball.name: Safari Ball
safari-ball.description: A special BALL that is used only in the SAFARI ZONE.
safari-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1.5×. This item can only be used in the great marsh or kanto safari zone.

net-ball.name: Net Ball
net-ball.description: A BALL that works well on WATER- and BUG-type POKéMON.
net-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If the wild Pokémon is water- or bug-type, this ball has a catch rate of 3×. Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 1×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

dive-ball.name: Dive Ball
dive-ball.description: A BALL that works better on POKéMON on the ocean floor.
dive-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If the wild Pokémon was encountered by surfing or fishing, this ball has a catch rate of 3.5×. Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 1×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

nest-ball.name: Nest Ball
nest-ball.description: A BALL that works better on weaker POKéMON.
nest-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. Has a catch rate of given by `(40 - level) / 10`, where `level` is the wild Pokémon’s level, to a maximum of 3.9× for level 1 Pokémon. If the wild Pokémon’s level is higher than 30, this ball has a catch rate of 1×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

repeat-ball.name: Repeat Ball
repeat-ball.description: A BALL that works better on POKéMON caught before.
repeat-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If the wild Pokémon’s species is marked as caught in the trainer’s Pokédex, this ball has a catch rate of 3×. Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 1×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

timer-ball.name: Timer Ball
timer-ball.description: More effective as more turns are taken in battle.
timer-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. Has a catch rate of 1.1× on the first turn of the battle and increases by 0.1× every turn, to a maximum of 4× on turn 30. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

luxury-ball.name: Luxury Ball
luxury-ball.description: A cozy BALL that makes POKéMON more friendly.
luxury-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×. Whenever the caught Pokémon’s happiness increases, it increases by one extra point. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

premier-ball.name: Premier Ball
premier-ball.description: A rare BALL made in commemoration of some event.
premier-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

dusk-ball.name: Dusk Ball
dusk-ball.description: A somewhat different Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch wild Pokémon at night or in dark places like caves.
dusk-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If it’s currently nighttime or the wild Pokémon was encountered while walking in a cave, this ball has a catch rate of 3.5×. Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 1×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

heal-ball.name: Heal Ball
heal-ball.description: A remedial Poké Ball that restores the caught Pokémon’s HP and eliminates any status problem.
heal-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×. The caught Pokémon’s HP is immediately restored, PP for all its moves is restored, and any status ailment is cured. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

quick-ball.name: Quick Ball
quick-ball.description: A somewhat different Poké Ball that provides a better catch rate if it is used at the start of a wild encounter.
quick-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 4× on the first turn of a battle, but 1× any other time. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

cherish-ball.name: Cherish Ball
cherish-ball.description: A quite rare Poké Ball that has been specially crafted to commemorate an occasion of some sort.
cherish-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

potion.name: Potion
potion.description: Restores the HP of a POKéMON by 20 points.
potion.effect: Used on a friendly Pokémon : Restores 20 HP.

antidote.name: Antidote
antidote.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures poison.

burn-heal.name: Burn Heal
burn-heal.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures a burn.

ice-heal.name: Ice Heal
ice-heal.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures freezing.

awakening.name: Awakening
awakening.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures sleep.

paralyze-heal.name: Paralyze Heal
paralyze-heal.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures paralysis.

full-restore.name: Full Restore
full-restore.description: Fully restores the HP and status of a POKéMON.
full-restore.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores HP to full and cures any status ailment and confusion.

max-potion.name: Max Potion
max-potion.description: Fully restores the HP of a POKéMON.
max-potion.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores HP to full.

hyper-potion.name: Hyper Potion
hyper-potion.description: Restores the HP of a POKéMON by 200 points.
hyper-potion.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 200 HP.

super-potion.name: Super Potion
super-potion.description: Restores the HP of a POKéMON by 50 points.
super-potion.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 50 HP.

full-heal.name: Full Heal
full-heal.description: Heals all the status problems of one POKéMON.
full-heal.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures any status ailment and confusion.

revive.name: Revive
revive.description: Revives a fainted POKéMON with half its HP.
revive.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Revives the Pokémon and restores half its HP.

max-revive.name: Max Revive
max-revive.description: Revives a fainted POKéMON with all its HP.
max-revive.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Revives the Pokémon and restores its HP to full.

fresh-water.name: Fresh Water
fresh-water.description: A mineral water that restores HP by 50 points.
fresh-water.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 50 HP.

soda-pop.name: Soda Pop
soda-pop.description: A fizzy soda drink that restores HP by 60 points.
soda-pop.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 60 HP.

lemonade.name: Lemonade
lemonade.description: A very sweet drink that restores HP by 80 points.
lemonade.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 80 HP.

moomoo-milk.name: Moomoo Milk
moomoo-milk.description: A nutritious milk that restores HP by 100 points.
moomoo-milk.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 100 HP.

energy-powder.name: Energy Powder
energy-powder.description: A bitter powder that restores HP by 50 points.
energy-powder.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 50 HP. Decreases happiness by 5/5/10.

energy-root.name: Energy Root
energy-root.description: A bitter root that restores HP by 200 points.
energy-root.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 200 HP. Decreases happiness by 10/10/15.

heal-powder.name: Heal Powder
heal-powder.description: A bitter powder that heals all status problems.
heal-powder.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures any status ailment. Decreases happiness by 5/5/10.

revival-herb.name: Revival Herb
revival-herb.description: A very bitter herb that revives a fainted POKéMON.
revival-herb.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Revives a fainted Pokémon and restores its HP to full. Decreases happiness by 10/10/15.

ether.name: Ether
ether.description: Restores the PP of a selected move by 10.
ether.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 10 PP for a selected move.

max-ether.name: Max Ether
max-ether.description: Fully restores the PP of a selected move.
max-ether.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores PP to full for a selected move.

elixir.name: Elixir
elixir.description: Restores the PP of all moves by 10.
elixir.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 10 PP for each move.

max-elixir.name: Max Elixir
max-elixir.description: Fully restores the PP of a POKéMON’s moves.
max-elixir.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores PP to full for each move.

lava-cookie.name: Lava Cookie
lava-cookie.description: A local specialty that heals all status problems.
lava-cookie.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures any status ailment and confusion.

berry-juice.name: Berry Juice
berry-juice.description: A 100% pure juice that restores HP by 20 points.
berry-juice.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 20 HP.

sacred-ash.name: Sacred Ash
sacred-ash.description: Fully revives and restores all fainted POKéMON.
sacred-ash.effect: Used : Revives all fainted Pokémon in the party and restores their HP to full.

hp-up.name: HP Up
hp-up.description: Raises the HP of one POKéMON.
hp-up.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases HP effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100. Increases happiness by 5/3/2.

protein.name: Protein
protein.description: Raises the stat ATTACK of one POKéMON.
protein.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases Attack effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100. Increases happiness by 5/3/2.

iron.name: Iron
iron.description: Raises the stat DEFENSE of one POKéMON.
iron.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases Defense effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100. Increases happiness by 5/3/2.

carbos.name: Carbos
carbos.description: Raises the stat SPEED of one POKéMON.
carbos.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases Speed effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100. Increases happiness by 5/3/2.

calcium.name: Calcium
calcium.description: Raises the stat SP. ATK of one POKéMON.
calcium.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases Special Attack effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100. Increases happiness by 5/3/2.

rare-candy.name: Rare Candy
rare-candy.description: Raises the level of a POKéMON by one.
rare-candy.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases level by 1. Increases happiness by 5/3/2.

pp-up.name: PP Up
pp-up.description: Raises the maximum PP of a selected move.
pp-up.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases a selected move’s max PP by 20% its original max PP, to a maximum of 1.6×. Increases happiness by 5/3/2.

zinc.name: Zinc
zinc.description: Raises the base SP. DEF stat of one POKéMON.
zinc.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases Special Defense effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100. Increases happiness by 5/3/2.

pp-max.name: PP Max
pp-max.description: Raises the PP of a move to its maximum points.
pp-max.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases a selected move’s max PP to 1.6× its original max PP. Increases happiness by 5/3/2.

old-gateau.name: Old Gateau
old-gateau.description: Old Chateau’s hidden specialty. It heals all the status problems of a single Pokémon.
old-gateau.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures any status ailment and confusion.

guard-spec.name: Guard Spec.
guard-spec.description: Prevents stat reduction when used in battle.
guard-spec.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Protects the target’s stats from being lowered for the next five turns. Increases happiness by 1/1/0.

dire-hit.name: Dire Hit
dire-hit.description: Raises the critical-hit ratio during one battle.
dire-hit.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Increases the target’s critical hit chance by one stage until it leaves the field. Increases happiness by 1/1/0.

x-attack.name: X Attack
x-attack.description: Raises the stat ATTACK during one battle.
x-attack.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Raises the target’s Attack by one stage. Increases happiness by 1/1/0.

x-defense.name: X Defense
x-defense.description: Raises the stat DEFENSE during one battle.
x-defense.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Raises the target’s Defense by one stage. Increases happiness by 1/1/0.

x-speed.name: X Speed
x-speed.description: Raises the stat SPEED during one battle.
x-speed.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Raises the target’s Speed by one stage. Increases happiness by 1/1/0.

x-accuracy.name: X Accuracy
x-accuracy.description: Raises accuracy of attack moves during one battle.
x-accuracy.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Raises the target’s accuracy by one stage. Increases happiness by 1/1/0.

x-sp-atk.name: X Sp. Atk
x-sp-atk.description: Raises the stat SP. ATK during one battle.
x-sp-atk.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Raises the target’s Special Attack by one stage. Increases happiness by 1/1/0.

x-sp-def.name: X Sp. Def
x-sp-def.description: An item that raises the Sp. Def stat of a Pokémon in battle. It wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.
x-sp-def.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Raises the target’s Special Defense by one stage. Increases happiness by 1/1/0.

poke-doll.name: Poké Doll
poke-doll.description: Use to flee from any battle with a wild POKéMON.
poke-doll.effect: Used in battle : Ends a wild battle. Cannot be used in trainer battles.

fluffy-tail.name: Fluffy Tail
fluffy-tail.description: Use to flee from any battle with a wild POKéMON.
fluffy-tail.effect: Used in battle : Ends a wild battle. Cannot be used in trainer battles.

blue-flute.name: Blue Flute
blue-flute.description: A glass flute that awakens sleeping POKéMON.
blue-flute.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures sleep.

yellow-flute.name: Yellow Flute
yellow-flute.description: A glass flute that snaps POKéMON out of confusion.
yellow-flute.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Cures confusion.

red-flute.name: Red Flute
red-flute.description: A glass flute that snaps POKéMON out of attraction.
red-flute.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Cures attraction.

black-flute.name: Black Flute
black-flute.description: A glass flute that keeps away wild POKéMON.
black-flute.effect: Used outside of battle : Decreases the wild Pokémon encounter rate by 50%.

white-flute.name: White Flute
white-flute.description: A glass flute that lures wild POKéMON.
white-flute.effect: Used outside of battle : Doubles the wild Pokémon encounter rate.

shoal-salt.name: Shoal Salt
shoal-salt.description: Salt obtained from deep inside the SHOAL CAVE.

shoal-shell.name: Shoal Shell
shoal-shell.description: A seashell that you found inside Shoal Cave.

red-shard.name: Red Shard
red-shard.description: A shard from an ancient item. Can be sold cheaply.
red-shard.effect: No effect. In Diamond and Pearl, trade ten for a sunny day TM in the house midway along the southern section of sinnoh route 212. In Platinum, trade to move tutors on sinnoh route 212, in snowpoint city, and in the survival area. Eight shards total are required per tutelage, but the particular combination of colors varies by move. In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a cheri berry, a leppa berry, and a pecha berry with the Juggler near the Pokémon Center in violet city. In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a persim berry, a pomeg berry, and a razz berry with the Juggler near the pal park entrance in fuchsia city.

blue-shard.name: Blue Shard
blue-shard.description: A shard from an ancient item. Can be sold cheaply.
blue-shard.effect: No effect. In Diamond and Pearl, trade ten for a rain dance TM in the house midway along the southern section of sinnoh route 212. In Platinum, trade to move tutors on sinnoh route 212, in snowpoint city, and in the survival area. Eight shards total are required per tutelage, but the particular combination of colors varies by move. In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a chesto berry, an oran berry, and a wiki berry with the Juggler near the Pokémon Center in violet city. In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a bluk berry, a cornn berry, and a kelpsy berry with the Juggler near the pal park entrance in fuchsia city.

yellow-shard.name: Yellow Shard
yellow-shard.description: A shard from an ancient item. Can be sold cheaply.
yellow-shard.effect: No effect. In Diamond and Pearl, trade ten for a sandstorm TM in the house midway along the southern section of sinnoh route 212. In Platinum, trade to move tutors on sinnoh route 212, in snowpoint city, and in the survival area. Eight shards total are required per tutelage, but the particular combination of colors varies by move. In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for an aspear berry, a iapapa berry, and a sitrus berry with the Juggler near the Pokémon Center in violet city. In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a grepa berry, a nomel berry, and a pinap berry with the Juggler near the pal park entrance in fuchsia city.

green-shard.name: Green Shard
green-shard.description: A shard from an ancient item. Can be sold cheaply.
green-shard.effect: No effect. In Diamond and Pearl, trade ten for a hail TM in the house midway along the southern section of sinnoh route 212. In Platinum, trade to move tutors on sinnoh route 212, in snowpoint city, and in the survival area. Eight shards total are required per tutelage, but the particular combination of colors varies by move. In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for an aguav berry, a lum berry, and a rawst berry with the Juggler near the Pokémon Center in violet city. In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a durin berry, a hondew berry, and a wepear berry with the Juggler near the pal park entrance in fuchsia city.

super-repel.name: Super Repel
super-repel.description: Repels weak wild POKéMON for 200 steps.
super-repel.effect: Used outside of battle : Trainer will skip encounters with wild Pokémon of a lower level than the lead party Pokémon. This effect wears off after the trainer takes 200 steps.

max-repel.name: Max Repel
max-repel.description: Repels weak wild POKéMON for 250 steps.
max-repel.effect: Used outside of battle : Trainer will skip encounters with wild Pokémon of a lower level than the lead party Pokémon. This effect wears off after the trainer takes 250 steps.

escape-rope.name: Escape Rope
escape-rope.description: Use to escape instantly from a cave or a dungeon.
escape-rope.effect: Used outside of battle : Transports the trainer to the last-entered dungeon entrance. Cannot be used outside, in buildings, or in distortion world, sinnoh hall of origin 1, spear pillar, or turnback cave.

repel.name: Repel
repel.description: Repels weak wild POKéMON for 100 steps.
repel.effect: Used outside of battle : Trainer will skip encounters with wild Pokémon of a lower level than the lead party Pokémon. This effect wears off after the trainer takes 100 steps.

sun-stone.name: Sun Stone
sun-stone.description: Makes certain species of POKéMON evolve.
sun-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves a cottonee into whimsicott, a gloom into bellossom, a petilil into lilligant, or a sunkern into sunflora.

moon-stone.name: Moon Stone
moon-stone.description: Makes certain species of POKéMON evolve.
moon-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves a clefairy into clefable, a jigglypuff into wigglytuff, a munna into musharna, a nidorina into nidoqueen, a nidorino into nidoking, or a skitty into delcatty.

fire-stone.name: Fire Stone
fire-stone.description: Makes certain species of POKéMON evolve.
fire-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves an eevee into flareon, a growlithe into arcanine, a pansear into simisear, or a vulpix into ninetales.

thunder-stone.name: Thunder Stone
thunder-stone.description: Makes certain species of POKéMON evolve.
thunder-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves an eelektrik into eelektross, an eevee into jolteon, or a pikachu into raichu.

water-stone.name: Water Stone
water-stone.description: Makes certain species of POKéMON evolve.
water-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves an eevee into vaporeon, a lombre into ludicolo, a panpour into simipour, a poliwhirl into poliwrath, a shellder into cloyster, or a staryu into starmie.

leaf-stone.name: Leaf Stone
leaf-stone.description: Makes certain species of POKéMON evolve.
leaf-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves an exeggcute into exeggutor, a gloom into vileplume, a nuzleaf into shiftry, a pansage into simisage, or a weepinbell into victreebel.

tiny-mushroom.name: Tiny Mushroom
tiny-mushroom.description: A plain, ordinary mushroom. Can be sold cheaply.

big-mushroom.name: Big Mushroom
big-mushroom.description: A rare mushroom that would sell at a high price.

pearl.name: Pearl
pearl.description: A pretty pearl. Can be sold cheaply.

big-pearl.name: Big Pearl
big-pearl.description: A lovely large pearl that would sell at a high price.

stardust.name: Stardust
stardust.description: Beautiful red sand. Can be sold at a high price.

star-piece.name: Star Piece
star-piece.description: A red gem shard. It would sell for a very high price.

nugget.name: Nugget
nugget.description: A nugget of pure gold. Can be sold at a high price.

heart-scale.name: Heart Scale
heart-scale.description: A lovely scale. It is coveted by collectors.
heart-scale.effect: Trade one to the Move Relearner near the shore in pastoria city or with the Move Deleter in blackthorn city to teach one party Pokémon a prior level-up move.

honey.name: Honey
honey.description: A sweet honey with a lush aroma that attracts wild Pokémon when it is used in grass, caves, or on special trees.
honey.effect: Used outside of battle : Immediately triggers a wild Pokémon battle, as long as the trainer is somewhere with wild Pokémon—i.e., in tall grass, in a cave, or surfing. Can be smeared on sweet-smelling trees to attract tree-dwelling Pokémon after six hours.

growth-mulch.name: Growth Mulch
growth-mulch.description: A plant fertilizer spread on soft soil. It speeds up the growth of Berries. However, it also dries the soil faster.
growth-mulch.effect: Used on a patch of soil : Plant’s growth stages will each last 25% less time. Dries soil out more quickly.

damp-mulch.name: Damp Mulch
damp-mulch.description: A plant fertilizer spread on soft soil. It slows the growth of Berries and causes the soil to dry slower.
damp-mulch.effect: Used on a patch of soil : Plant’s growth stages will each last 25% more time. Dries soil out more slowly.

stable-mulch.name: Stable Mulch
stable-mulch.description: A plant fertilizer spread on soft soil. It extends the time ripened Berries remain on their plants before falling.
stable-mulch.effect: Used on a patch of soil : Fully-grown plant will last 25% longer before dying and possibly regrowing.

gooey-mulch.name: Gooey Mulch
gooey-mulch.description: A plant fertilizer spread on soft soil. It ups the number of times new plants grow where mature plants withered.
gooey-mulch.effect: Used on a path of soil : Plant will regrow after dying 25% more times.

root-fossil.name: Root Fossil
root-fossil.description: A fossil of an ancient, seafloor- dwelling POKéMON.
root-fossil.effect: Give to a scientist in the mining museum in oreburgh city or the Museum of Science in pewter city to receive a lileep.

claw-fossil.name: Claw Fossil
claw-fossil.description: A fossil of an ancient, seafloor- dwelling POKéMON.
claw-fossil.effect: Give to a scientist in the mining museum in oreburgh city or the Museum of Science in pewter city to receive an anorith.

helix-fossil.name: Helix Fossil
helix-fossil.description: A piece of an ancient marine POKéMON’s seashell.
helix-fossil.effect: Give to a scientist in the mining museum in oreburgh city or the Museum of Science in pewter city to receive an omanyte.

dome-fossil.name: Dome Fossil
dome-fossil.description: A piece of an ancient marine POKéMON’s shell.
dome-fossil.effect: Give to a scientist in the mining museum in oreburgh city or the Museum of Science in pewter city to receive a kabuto.

old-amber.name: Old Amber
old-amber.description: A stone containing the genes of an ancient POKéMON.
old-amber.effect: Give to a scientist in the mining museum in oreburgh city or the Museum of Science in pewter city to receive an aerodactyl.

armor-fossil.name: Armor Fossil
armor-fossil.description: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that lived on the land. It appears to be part of a collar.
armor-fossil.effect: Give to a scientist in the mining museum in oreburgh city or the Museum of Science in pewter city to receive a shieldon.

skull-fossil.name: Skull Fossil
skull-fossil.description: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that lived on the land. It appears to be part of a head.
skull-fossil.effect: Give to a scientist in the mining museum in oreburgh city or the Museum of Science in pewter city to receive a cranidos.

rare-bone.name: Rare Bone
rare-bone.description: A bone that is extremely valuable for Pokémon archaeology. It can be sold for a high price to shops.

shiny-stone.name: Shiny Stone
shiny-stone.description: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It shines with a dazzling light.
shiny-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves a minccino into cinccino, a roselia into roserade, or a togetic into togekiss.

dusk-stone.name: Dusk Stone
dusk-stone.description: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is as dark as dark can be.
dusk-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves a lampent into chandelure, a misdreavus into mismagius, or a murkrow into honchkrow.

dawn-stone.name: Dawn Stone
dawn-stone.description: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It sparkles like eyes.
dawn-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves a male kirlia into gallade or a female snorunt into froslass.

oval-stone.name: Oval Stone
oval-stone.description: A peculiar stone that makes certain species of Pokémon evolve. It is shaped like an egg.
oval-stone.effect: Held by happiny : Holder evolves into chansey when it levels up during the daytime.

odd-keystone.name: Odd Keystone
odd-keystone.description: A vital item that is needed to keep a stone tower from collapsing. Voices can be heard from it occasionally.
odd-keystone.effect: Place in the tower on sinnoh route 209. Check the stone to encounter a spiritomb, as long as the trainer’s Underground status card counts at least 32 greetings.

adamant-orb.effect: Held by dialga : Holder’s dragon- and steel-type moves have 1.2× their usual power.

grass-mail.name: Grass Mail
grass-mail.description: Stationery featuring a print of a refreshingly green field. Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.
grass-mail.effect: Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading. Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon. Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC. Held : Holder cannot be placed in the PC. Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.

cheri-berry.name: Cheri Berry
cheri-berry.description: A hold item that heals paralysis in battle.
cheri-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder is paralyzed, it consumes this item to cure the paralysis. Used on a party Pokémon : Cures paralysis.

chesto-berry.name: Chesto Berry
chesto-berry.description: A hold item that awakens POKéMON in battle.
chesto-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder is asleep, it consumes this item to wake up. Used on a party Pokémon : Cures sleep.

pecha-berry.name: Pecha Berry
pecha-berry.description: A hold item that heals poisoning in battle.
pecha-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder is poisoned, it consumes this item to cure the poison. Used on a party Pokémon : Cures poison.

rawst-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder is burned, it consumes this item to cure the burn. Used on a party Pokémon : Cures a burn.

aspear-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder is frozen, it consumes this item to thaw itself. Used on a party Pokémon : Cures freezing.

leppa-berry.description: A hold item that restores 10 PP in battle.
leppa-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder is out of PP for one of its moves, it consumes this item to restore 10 of that move’s PP. Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 10 PP for a selected move.

oran-berry.description: A hold item that restores 10 HP in battle.
oran-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 10 HP. Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 10 HP.

persim-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder is confused, it consumes this item to cure the confusion. Used on a party Pokémon : Cures confusion.

lum-berry.description: A hold item that heals status in battle.
lum-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder is afflicted with a major status ailment, it consumes this item to cure the ailment. Used on a party Pokémon : Cures any major status ailment.

sitrus-berry.name: Sitrus Berry
sitrus-berry.description: A hold item that restores 30 HP in battle.
sitrus-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 1/4 its max HP. Used on a party Pokémon : Restores 1/4 the Pokémon’s max HP.

figy-berry.description: A hold item that restores HP but may confuse.
figy-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 1/8 its max HP. If the holder dislikes spicy flavors (i.e., has a nature that lowers Attack), it will also become confused.

starf-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to raise a random stat by two stages.

enigma-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder takes super-effective damage, it consumes this item to restore 1/4 its max HP.

micle-berry.name: Micle Berry
micle-berry.description: A Poffin ingredient. If held by a Pokémon, it raises the accuracy of a move just once in a pinch.
micle-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item, and its next used move has 1.2× its normal accuracy.

custap-berry.name: Custap Berry
custap-berry.description: A Poffin ingredient. If held by a Pokémon, it gets to move first just once in a pinch.
custap-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item. On the following turn, the holder will act first among moves with the same priority, regardless of Speed.

jaboca-berry.name: Jaboca Berry
jaboca-berry.description: A Poffin ingredient. If held by a Pokémon, and if a foe’s physical attack lands, the foe also takes damage.
jaboca-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder takes physical damage, it consumes this item to damage the attacking Pokémon for 1/8 its max HP.

rowap-berry.name: Rowap Berry
rowap-berry.description: A Poffin ingredient. If held by a Pokémon, and if a foe’s special attack lands, the foe also takes damage.
rowap-berry.effect: Held in battle : When the holder takes special damage, it consumes this item to damage the attacking Pokémon for 1/8 its max HP.

bright-powder.name: Bright Powder
bright-powder.description: A hold item that casts a glare to reduce accuracy.
bright-powder.effect: Held in battle : Moves targeting the holder have 0.9× chance to hit.

white-herb.name: White Herb
white-herb.description: A hold item that restores any lowered stat.
white-herb.effect: Held in battle : At the end of each turn, if any of the holder’s stats have a negative stat modifier, the holder consumes this item to remove the modifiers from those stats.

macho-brace.name: Macho Brace
macho-brace.description: A hold item that promotes growth, but reduces SPEED.
macho-brace.effect: Held : When the holder would gain effort due to battle, it gains double that effort instead. Held in battle : Holder has half its Speed.

exp-share.name: Exp. Share
exp-share.description: A hold item that gets EXP. points from battles.
exp-share.effect: Held : Experience is split across two groups: Pokémon who participated in battle, and Pokémon holding this item. Each Pokémon earns experience as though it had battled alone, divided by the number of Pokémon in its group, then divided by the number of groups. Pokémon holding this item who also participated in battle effectively earn experience twice. Fainted Pokémon never earn experience, and empty groups are ignored; thus, if a single Pokémon is holding this item and the only Pokémon who battled faints from explosion, the holder will gain full experience.

quick-claw.name: Quick Claw
quick-claw.description: A hold item that occasionally allows the first strike.
quick-claw.effect: Held in battle : Whenever the holder attempts to use a move, it has a 3/16 chance to act first among moves with the same priority. If multiple Pokémon have this effect at the same time, Speed is the tie-breaker as normal, but the effect of trick room is ignored.

soothe-bell.name: Soothe Bell
soothe-bell.description: A hold item that calms spirits and fosters friendship.
soothe-bell.effect: Held : When the holder would earn happiness for any reason, it earns twice that amount instead.

mental-herb.name: Mental Herb
mental-herb.description: A hold item that snaps POKéMON out of infatuation.
mental-herb.effect: Held in battle : When the holder is attracted, it consumes this item to cure the attraction.

choice-band.name: Choice Band
choice-band.description: Raises a move’s power, but permits only that move.
choice-band.effect: Held in battle : Holder has 1.5× its Attack. When the holder attempts to use a move, all its other moves are disabled until it leaves battle or loses this item. The restriction ends even if this item is swapped for another Choice item via trick or switcheroo.

kings-rock.name: King’s Rock
kings-rock.description: A hold item that may cause flinching when the foe is hit.
kings-rock.effect: Held in battle : Holder’s damaging moves have a 10% chance to make their target flinch. This chance applies independently to each hit of a multi-hit move. This item’s chance is rolled independently of any other move effects; e.g., a move with a 30% chance to flinch normally will have a 37% total chance to flinch when used with this item, because 3% of the time, both effects activate. Held by poliwhirl or slowbro : Holder evolves into politoed or slowking, respectively, when traded.

silver-powder.name: Silver Powder
silver-powder.description: A hold item that raises the power of BUG-type moves.
silver-powder.effect: Held in battle : Holder’s bug-type moves have 1.2× their power.

amulet-coin.name: Amulet Coin
amulet-coin.description: Doubles money in battle if the holder takes part.
amulet-coin.effect: Held : If the holder participated in a trainer battle, the trainer earns twice the usual prize money. This effect applies even if the holder fainted. This effect does not stack with any other similar effect.

cleanse-tag.name: Cleanse Tag
cleanse-tag.description: A hold item that helps repel wild POKéMON.
cleanse-tag.effect: Held by lead Pokémon: Prevents wild battles with Pokémon that are lower level than the holder.

soul-dew.name: Soul Dew
soul-dew.description: Hold item: Raises SP. ATK & SP. DEF of LATIOS & LATIAS.
soul-dew.effect: Held by Latias or Latios: Increases the holder’s Special Attack and Special Defense by 50%.

stick.effect: Held by Farfetch’d: Raises the holder’s critical hit counter by 2.

wide-lens.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a magnifying lens that slightly boosts the accuracy of moves.
wide-lens.effect: Held: Increases the accuracy of any move the holder uses by 10% (multiplied; i.e. 70% accuracy is increased to 77%).

muscle-band.name: Muscle Band
muscle-band.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a headband that slightly boosts the power of physical moves.
muscle-band.effect: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s physical moves by 10%.

wise-glasses.name: Wise Glasses
wise-glasses.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a thick pair of glasses that slightly boosts the power of special moves.
wise-glasses.effect: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s special moves by 10%.

expert-belt.name: Expert Belt
expert-belt.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a well-worn belt that slightly boosts the power of supereffective moves.
expert-belt.effect: Held: When the holder hits with a super-effective move, its power is raised by 20%.

light-clay.name: Light Clay
light-clay.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of barrier moves like Light Screen and Reflect used by the holder.
light-clay.effect: Held: The holder’s Reflect and Light Screen will create effects lasting for eight turns rather than five. As this item affects the move rather than the barrier itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.

life-orb.name: Life Orb
life-orb.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts the power of moves, but at the cost of some HP on each hit.
life-orb.effect: Held: Damage from the holder’s moves is increased by 30%. On each turn the holder uses a damage-inflicting move, it takes 10% its max HP in damage.

power-herb.name: Power Herb
power-herb.description: A single-use item to be held by a Pokémon. It allows the immediate use of a move that charges on the first turn.
power-herb.effect: Held: Whenever the holder uses a move that requires a turn to charge first (Bounce, Dig, Dive, Fly, Razor Wind, Skull Bash, Sky Attack, or Solarbeam), this item is consumed and the charge is skipped. Skull Bash still provides a Defense boost.

toxic-orb.name: Toxic Orb
toxic-orb.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a bizarre orb that badly poisons the holder in battle.
toxic-orb.effect: Held: Badly poisons the holder at the end of each turn.

flame-orb.name: Flame Orb
flame-orb.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a bizarre orb that inflicts a burn on the holder in battle.
flame-orb.effect: Held: Burns the holder at the end of each turn.

quick-powder.name: Quick Powder
quick-powder.description: An item to be held by DITTO. Extremely fine yet hard, this odd powder boosts the Speed stat.
quick-powder.effect: Held by Ditto: Doubles the holder’s initial Speed.

focus-sash.name: Focus Sash
focus-sash.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If it has full HP, the holder will endure one potential KO attack, leaving 1 HP.
focus-sash.effect: Held: If the holder has full HP and is attacked for regular damage that would faint it, this item is consumed and prevents the holder’s HP from lowering below 1. This effect works against multi-hit attacks, but does not work against the effects of Doom Desire or Future Sight.

zoom-lens.name: Zoom Lens
zoom-lens.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder moves after the foe, its critical-hit ratio will be boosted.
zoom-lens.effect: Held: Raises the holder’s Accuracy by 20% when it goes last. Ingame description is incorrect.

metronome.name: Metronome
metronome.description: A Pokémon hold item that boosts a move used consecutively. Its effect is reset if another move is used.
metronome.effect: Held: Each time the holder uses the same move consecutively, its power is increased by another 10% of its original, to a maximum of 100%.

iron-ball.name: Iron Ball
iron-ball.description: A Pokémon hold item that cuts Speed. It makes Flying-type and levitating holders susceptible to Ground moves.
iron-ball.effect: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%. If the holder is Flying or has Levitate, it takes regular damage from Ground attacks and is suspectible to Spikes and Toxic Spikes.

lagging-tail.name: Lagging Tail
lagging-tail.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is tremendously heavy and makes the holder move slower than usual.
lagging-tail.effect: Held: The holder will go last within its move’s priority bracket, regardless of Speed. If multiple Pokémon within the same priority bracket are subject to this effect, the slower Pokémon will go first. The holder will move after Pokémon with Stall. If the holder has Stall, Stall is ignored. This item ignores Trick Room.

destiny-knot.name: Destiny Knot
destiny-knot.description: A long, thin, bright red string to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder becomes infatuated, the foe does too.
destiny-knot.effect: Held: When the holder becomes Attracted, the Pokémon it is Attracted to becomes Attracted back.

black-sludge.name: Black Sludge
black-sludge.description: A hold item that gradually restores the HP of Poison-type Pokémon. It inflicts damage on all other types.
black-sludge.effect: Held: If the holder is Poison-type, restores 1/16 max HP at the end of each turn. Otherwise, damages the holder by 1/16 its max HP at the end of each turn.

icy-rock.name: Icy Rock
icy-rock.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Hail used by the holder.
icy-rock.effect: Held: The holder’s Hail will create a hailstorm lasting for eight turns rather than five. As this item affects the move rather than the weather itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.

smooth-rock.name: Smooth Rock
smooth-rock.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Sandstorm used by the holder.
smooth-rock.effect: Held: The holder’s Sandstorm will create a sandstorm lasting for eight turns rather than five. As this item affects the move rather than the weather itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.

heat-rock.name: Heat Rock
heat-rock.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Sunny Day used by the holder.
heat-rock.effect: Held: The holder’s Sunny Day will create sunshine lasting for eight turns rather than five. As this item affects the move rather than the weather itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.

damp-rock.name: Damp Rock
damp-rock.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Rain Dance used by the holder.
damp-rock.effect: Held: The holder’s Rain Dance will create rain lasting for eight turns rather than five. As this item affects the move rather than the weather itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.

grip-claw.name: Grip Claw
grip-claw.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of multiturn attacks like Bind and Wrap.
grip-claw.effect: Held: Increases the duration of the holder’s multiturn (2-5 turn) moves by three turns.

choice-scarf.name: Choice Scarf
choice-scarf.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This scarf boosts Speed, but allows the use of only one kind of move.
choice-scarf.effect: Held: Increases the holder’s Speed by 50%, but restricts it to the first move it uses until it leaves battle or loses this item. If this item is swapped for another Choice item via Trick or Switcheroo, the holder’s restriction is still lifted, but it will again be restricted to the next move it uses. (Quirk: If the holder is switched in by U-Turn and it also knows U-Turn, U-Turn becomes its restricted move.)

sticky-barb.name: Sticky Barb
sticky-barb.description: A hold item that damages the holder on every turn. It may latch on to foes that touch the holder.
sticky-barb.effect: Held: Damaged the holder for 1/8 its max HP. When the holder is struck by a contact move, damages the attacker for 1/8 its max HP; if the attacker is not holding an item, it will take this item.

power-bracer.name: Power Bracer
power-bracer.description: A Pokémon hold item that promotes Attack gain on leveling, but reduces the Speed stat.
power-bracer.effect: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%. Whenever the holder gains Attack effort from battle, increases that effort by 4; this applies before the PokéRUS doubling effect.

power-belt.name: Power Belt
power-belt.description: A Pokémon hold item that promotes Defense gain on leveling, but reduces the Speed stat.
power-belt.effect: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%. Whenever the holder gains Defense effort from battle, increases that effort by 4; this applies before the PokéRUS doubling effect.

luck-incense.effect: Held: Doubles the money the trainer receives after an in-game trainer battle. This effect cannot apply more than once to the same battle.

pure-incense.name: Pure Incense
pure-incense.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It helps keep wild Pokémon away if the holder is the first one in the party.
pure-incense.effect: Held by lead Pokémon: Prevents wild battles with Pokémon that are lower level than the holder.

protector.name: Protector
protector.description: A protective item of some sort. It is extremely stiff and heavy. It is loved by a certain Pokémon.
protector.effect: Held by Rhydon: Evolves the holder into Rhyperior when traded.

electirizer.description: A box packed with a tremendous amount of electric energy. It is loved by a certain Pokémon.
electirizer.effect: Held by Electabuzz: Evolves the holder into Electivire when traded.

magmarizer.name: Magmarizer
magmarizer.description: A box packed with a tremendous amount of magma energy. It is loved by a certain Pokémon.
magmarizer.effect: Held by Magmar: Evolves the holder into Magmortar when traded.

dubious-disc.name: Dubious Disc

dubious-disc.effect: Held by Porygon2: Evolves the holder into Porygon-Z when traded.

razor-claw.name: Razor Claw
