razor-fang.effect: Held: When the holder attacks with most damaging moves, provides an extra 11.7% (30/256) chance for the target to flinch. Held by Gligar: Evolves the holder into Gliscor when it levels up.

tm01.name: TM01
tm01.description: Powerful, but makes the user flinch if hit by the foe.
tm01.effect: Teaches Focus Punch to a compatible Pokémon.

tm02.name: TM02
tm02.description: Hooks and slashes the foe with long, sharp claws.
tm02.effect: Teaches Dragon Claw to a compatible Pokémon.

tm03.name: TM03
tm03.description: Generates an ultrasonic wave that may confuse.
tm03.effect: Teaches Water Pulse to a compatible Pokémon.

tm04.name: TM04
tm04.description: Raises SP. ATK and SP. DEF by focusing the mind.
tm04.effect: Teaches Calm Mind to a compatible Pokémon.

tm05.name: TM05
tm05.description: A savage roar that makes the foe flee to end the battle.
tm05.effect: Teaches Roar to a compatible Pokémon.

tm06.name: TM06
tm06.description: Poisons the foe with a toxin that gradually worsens.
tm06.effect: Teaches Toxic to a compatible Pokémon.

tm07.name: TM07
tm07.description: Summons a hailstorm that hurts all types except ICE.
tm07.effect: Teaches Hail to a compatible Pokémon.

tm08.name: TM08
tm08.description: Bulks up the body to boost both ATTACK & DEFENSE.
tm08.effect: Teaches Bulk Up to a compatible Pokémon.

tm09.name: TM09
tm09.description: Shoots 2 to 5 seeds in a row to strike the foe.
tm09.effect: Teaches Bullet Seed to a compatible Pokémon.

tm10.name: TM10
tm10.description: The attack power varies among different POKéMON.
tm10.effect: Teaches Hidden Power to a compatible Pokémon.

tm11.name: TM11
tm11.description: Raises the power of FIRE-type moves for 5 turns.
tm11.effect: Teaches Sunny Day to a compatible Pokémon.

tm12.name: TM12
tm12.description: Enrages the foe so it can only use attack moves.
tm12.effect: Teaches Taunt to a compatible Pokémon.

tm13.name: TM13
tm13.description: Fires an icy cold beam that may freeze the foe.
tm13.effect: Teaches Ice Beam to a compatible Pokémon.

tm14.name: TM14
tm14.description: A vicious snow-and- wind attack that may freeze the foe.
tm14.effect: Teaches Blizzard to a compatible Pokémon.

tm15.name: TM15
tm15.description: Powerful, but needs recharging the next turn.
tm15.effect: Teaches Hyper Beam to a compatible Pokémon.

tm16.name: TM16
tm16.description: Creates a wall of light that lowers SP. ATK damage.
tm16.effect: Teaches Light Screen to a compatible Pokémon.

tm17.name: TM17
tm17.description: Negates all damage, but may fail if used in succession.
tm17.effect: Teaches Protect to a compatible Pokémon.

tm18.name: TM18
tm18.description: Raises the power of WATER-type moves for 5 turns.
tm18.effect: Teaches Rain Dance to a compatible Pokémon.

tm19.name: TM19
tm19.description: Recovers half the HP of the damage this move inflicts.
tm19.effect: Teaches Giga Drain to a compatible Pokémon.

tm20.name: TM20
tm20.description: Prevents status abnormality with a mystical power.
tm20.effect: Teaches Safeguard to a compatible Pokémon.

tm21.name: TM21
tm21.description: The less the user likes you, the more powerful this move.
tm21.effect: Teaches Frustration to a compatible Pokémon.

tm22.name: TM22
tm22.description: Absorbs sunlight in the 1st turn, then attacks next turn.
tm22.effect: Teaches SolarBeam to a compatible Pokémon.

tm23.name: TM23
tm23.description: Slams the foe with a hard tail. It may lower DEFENSE.
tm23.effect: Teaches Iron Tail to a compatible Pokémon.

tm24.name: TM24
tm24.description: A powerful electric attack that may cause paralysis.
tm24.effect: Teaches Thunderbolt to a compatible Pokémon.

tm25.name: TM25
tm25.description: Strikes the foe with a thunderbolt. It may paralyze.
tm25.effect: Teaches Thunder to a compatible Pokémon.

tm26.name: TM26
tm26.description: Causes a quake that has no effect on flying foes.
tm26.effect: Teaches Earthquake to a compatible Pokémon.

tm27.name: TM27
tm27.description: The more the user likes you, the more powerful this move.
tm27.effect: Teaches Return to a compatible Pokémon.

tm28.name: TM28
tm28.description: Digs underground the 1st turn, then strikes next turn.
tm28.effect: Teaches Dig to a compatible Pokémon.

tm29.name: TM29
tm29.description: A powerful psychic attack that may lower SP. DEF.
tm29.effect: Teaches Psychic to a compatible Pokémon.

tm30.name: TM30
tm30.description: Hurls a dark lump at the foe. It may lower SP. DEF.
tm30.effect: Teaches Shadow Ball to a compatible Pokémon.

tm31.name: TM31
tm31.description: Destroys barriers like LIGHT SCREEN and causes damage.
tm31.effect: Teaches Brick Break to a compatible Pokémon.

tm32.name: TM32
tm32.description: Creates illusory copies to enhance elusiveness.
tm32.effect: Teaches Double Team to a compatible Pokémon.

tm33.name: TM33
tm33.description: Creates a wall of light that weakens physical attacks.
tm33.effect: Teaches Reflect to a compatible Pokémon.

tm34.name: TM34
tm34.description: Zaps the foe with a jolt of electricity that never misses.
tm34.effect: Teaches Shock Wave to a compatible Pokémon.

tm35.name: TM35
tm35.description: Looses a stream of fire that may burn the foe.
tm35.effect: Teaches Flamethrower to a compatible Pokémon.

tm36.name: TM36
tm36.description: Hurls sludge at the foe. It may poison the foe.
tm36.effect: Teaches Sludge Bomb to a compatible Pokémon.

tm37.name: TM37
tm37.description: Causes a sandstorm that hits the foe over several turns.
tm37.effect: Teaches Sandstorm to a compatible Pokémon.

tm38.name: TM38
tm38.description: A powerful fire attack that may burn the foe.
tm38.effect: Teaches Fire Blast to a compatible Pokémon.

tm39.name: TM39
tm39.description: Stops the foe from moving with rocks. May lower SPEED.
tm39.effect: Teaches Rock Tomb to a compatible Pokémon.

tm40.name: TM40
tm40.description: An extremely fast attack that can’t be avoided.
tm40.effect: Teaches Aerial Ace to a compatible Pokémon.

tm41.name: TM41
tm41.description: Prevents the foe from using the same move in a row.
tm41.effect: Teaches Torment to a compatible Pokémon.

tm42.name: TM42
tm42.description: Raises ATTACK when poisoned, burned, or paralyzed.
tm42.effect: Teaches Facade to a compatible Pokémon.

tm43.name: TM43
tm43.description: Adds an effect to attack depending on the location.
tm43.effect: Teaches Secret Power to a compatible Pokémon.

tm44.name: TM44
tm44.description: The user sleeps for 2 turns to restore health and status.
tm44.effect: Teaches Rest to a compatible Pokémon.

tm45.name: TM45
tm45.description: Makes it tough to attack a foe of the opposite gender.
tm45.effect: Teaches Attract to a compatible Pokémon.

tm46.name: TM46
tm46.description: While attacking, it may steal the foe’s held item.
tm46.effect: Teaches Thief to a compatible Pokémon.

tm47.name: TM47
tm47.description: Spreads hard-edged wings and slams into the foe.
tm47.effect: Teaches Steel Wing to a compatible Pokémon.

tm48.name: TM48
tm48.description: Switches abilities with the foe on the turn this is used.
tm48.effect: Teaches Skill Swap to a compatible Pokémon.

tm49.name: TM49
tm49.description: Steals the effects of the move the foe is trying to use.
tm49.effect: Teaches Snatch to a compatible Pokémon.

tm50.name: TM50
tm50.description: Enables full-power attack, but sharply lowers SP. ATK.
tm50.effect: Teaches Overheat to a compatible Pokémon.

tm51.name: TM51
tm51.description: The user lands and rests its body. It restores the user’s HP by up to half of its top HP.
tm51.effect: Teaches Roost to a compatible Pokémon.

tm52.name: TM52
tm52.description: The user attacks at full power. It may also lower the foe’s Sp. Def stat if it hits.
tm52.effect: Teaches Focus Blast to a compatible Pokémon.

tm53.name: TM53
tm53.description: The user draws power from nature and fires it at the foe. It may also lower the target’s Sp. Def.
tm53.effect: Teaches Energy Ball to a compatible Pokémon.

tm54.name: TM54
tm54.description: A restrained attack that prevents the foe from fainting. The target is left with at least 1 HP.
tm54.effect: Teaches False Swipe to a compatible Pokémon.

tm55.name: TM55
tm55.description: If the foe’s HP is down to under half, this attack will hit with double the power.
tm55.effect: Teaches Brine to a compatible Pokémon.

tm56.name: TM56
tm56.description: The user flings its held item at the foe to attack. Its power and effects depend on the item.
tm56.effect: Teaches Fling to a compatible Pokémon.

tm57.name: TM57
tm57.description: The user attacks with an electric charge. The user may use any remaining electricity to raise its Sp. Atk stat.
tm57.effect: Teaches Charge Beam to a compatible Pokémon.

tm58.name: TM58
tm58.description: The user endures any attack, leaving 1 HP. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.
tm58.effect: Teaches Endure to a compatible Pokémon.

tm59.name: TM59
tm59.description: The foe is attacked with a shock wave generated by the user’s gaping mouth.
tm59.effect: Teaches Dragon Pulse to a compatible Pokémon.

tm60.name: TM60
tm60.description: An energy-draining punch. The user’s HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.
tm60.effect: Teaches Drain Punch to a compatible Pokémon.

tm61.name: TM61
tm61.description: The user shoots a sinister, bluish white flame at the foe to inflict a burn.
tm61.effect: Teaches Will-O-Wisp to a compatible Pokémon.

tm62.name: TM62
tm62.description: The foe is attacked with powdery scales blown by wind. It may also raise all the user’s stats.
tm62.effect: Teaches Silver Wind to a compatible Pokémon.

tm63.name: TM63
tm63.description: It prevents the foe from using its held item. Its Trainer is also prevented from using items on it.
tm63.effect: Teaches Embargo to a compatible Pokémon.

tm64.name: TM64
tm64.description: The user explodes to inflict damage on all Pokémon in battle. The user faints upon using this move.
tm64.effect: Teaches Explosion to a compatible Pokémon.

tm65.name: TM65
tm65.description: The user slashes with a sharp claw made from shadows. It has a high critical-hit ratio.
tm65.effect: Teaches Shadow Claw to a compatible Pokémon.

tm66.name: TM66
tm66.description: The user stores power, then attacks. If the user can use this attack after the foe, its power is doubled.
tm66.effect: Teaches Payback to a compatible Pokémon.

tm67.name: TM67
tm67.description: The user recycles a single-use item that has been used in battle so it can be used again.
tm67.effect: Teaches Recycle to a compatible Pokémon.

tm68.name: TM68
tm68.description: The user charges the foe using every bit of its power. It must rest on the next turn to recover.
tm68.effect: Teaches Giga Impact to a compatible Pokémon.

tm69.name: TM69
tm69.description: The user polishes its body to reduce drag. It sharply raises the Speed stat.
tm69.effect: Teaches Rock Polish to a compatible Pokémon.

tm70.name: TM70
tm70.description: The user flashes a light that cuts the foe’s accuracy. It can also be used to illuminate caves.
tm70.effect: Teaches Flash to a compatible Pokémon.

tm71.name: TM71
tm71.description: The user stabs the foe with a sharpened stone from below. It has a high critical-hit ratio.
tm71.effect: Teaches Stone Edge to a compatible Pokémon.

tm72.name: TM72
tm72.description: An attack move that inflicts double the damage if the user has been hurt by the foe in the same turn.
tm72.effect: Teaches Avalanche to a compatible Pokémon.

tm73.name: TM73
tm73.description: A weak electric charge is launched at the foe. It causes paralysis if it hits.
tm73.effect: Teaches Thunder Wave to a compatible Pokémon.

tm74.name: TM74
tm74.description: The user tackles the foe from a high-speed spin. The slower the user, the greater the damage.
tm74.effect: Teaches Gyro Ball to a compatible Pokémon.

tm75.name: TM75
tm75.description: A frenetic dance to uplift the fighting spirit. It sharply raises the user’s Attack stat.
tm75.effect: Teaches Swords Dance to a compatible Pokémon.

tm76.name: TM76
tm76.description: The user lays a trap of levitating stones around the foe. The trap hurts foes that switch into battle.
tm76.effect: Teaches Stealth Rock to a compatible Pokémon.

tm77.name: TM77
tm77.description: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the foe.
tm77.effect: Teaches Psych Up to a compatible Pokémon.

tm78.name: TM78
tm78.description: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.
tm78.effect: Teaches Captivate to a compatible Pokémon.

tm79.name: TM79
tm79.description: The user releases a horrible aura imbued with dark thoughts. It may also make the target flinch.
tm79.effect: Teaches Dark Pulse to a compatible Pokémon.

tm80.name: TM80
tm80.description: Large boulders are hurled at the foe to inflict damage. It may also make the target flinch.
tm80.effect: Teaches Rock Slide to a compatible Pokémon.

tm81.name: TM81
tm81.description: The user slashes at the foe by crossing its scythes or claws as if they were a pair of scissors.
tm81.effect: Teaches X-Scissor to a compatible Pokémon.

tm82.name: TM82
tm82.description: While it is asleep, the user randomly uses one of the moves it knows.
tm82.effect: Teaches Sleep Talk to a compatible Pokémon.

tm83.name: TM83
tm83.description: The user draws power from the Berry it is holding to attack. The Berry determines its type and power.
tm83.effect: Teaches Natural Gift to a compatible Pokémon.

tm84.name: TM84
tm84.description: The foe is stabbed with a tentacle or arm seeped with poison. It may also poison the foe.
tm84.effect: Teaches Poison Jab to a compatible Pokémon.

tm85.name: TM85
tm85.description: An attack that works only on a sleeping foe. It absorbs half the damage caused to heal the user’s HP.
tm85.effect: Teaches Dream Eater to a compatible Pokémon.

tm86.name: TM86
tm86.description: The user snares the foe with grass and trips it. The heavier the foe, the greater the damage.
tm86.effect: Teaches Grass Knot to a compatible Pokémon.

tm87.name: TM87
tm87.description: The user enrages the foe into confusion. However, it also sharply raises the foe’s Attack stat.
tm87.effect: Teaches Swagger to a compatible Pokémon.

tm88.name: TM88
tm88.description: The user pecks the foe. If the foe is holding a Berry, the user plucks it and gains its effect.
tm88.effect: Teaches Pluck to a compatible Pokémon.

tm89.name: TM89
tm89.description: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.
tm89.effect: Teaches U-Turn to a compatible Pokémon.

tm90.name: TM90
tm90.description: The user makes a copy of itself using some of its HP. The copy serves as the user’s decoy.
tm90.effect: Teaches Substitute to a compatible Pokémon.

tm91.name: TM91
tm91.description: The user gathers all its light energy and releases it in one shot. It may also lower the foe’s Sp. Def stat.
tm91.effect: Teaches Flash Cannon to a compatible Pokémon.

tm92.name: TM92
tm92.description: The user creates a bizarre space in which slower Pokémon get to move first for five turns.
tm92.effect: Teaches Trick Room to a compatible Pokémon.

hm01.name: HM01
hm01.description: Attacks the foe with sharp blades or claws.
hm01.effect: Teaches Cut to a compatible Pokémon.

hm02.name: HM02
hm02.description: Flies up on the first turn, then attacks next turn.
hm02.effect: Teaches Fly to a compatible Pokémon.

hm03.name: HM03
hm03.description: Creates a huge wave, then crashes it down on the foe.
hm03.effect: Teaches Surf to a compatible Pokémon.

hm04.name: HM04
hm04.description: Builds enormous power, then slams the foe.
hm04.effect: Teaches Strength to a compatible Pokémon.

hm05.name: HM05
hm05.description: Looses a powerful blast of light that reduces accuracy.
hm05.effect: Teaches Defog to a compatible Pokémon.

hm06.name: HM06
hm06.description: A rock-crushingly tough attack that may lower DEFENSE.
hm06.effect: Teaches Rock Smash to a compatible Pokémon.

hm07.name: HM07
hm07.description: Attacks the foe with enough power to climb waterfalls.
hm07.effect: Teaches Waterfall to a compatible Pokémon.

hm08.name: HM08
hm08.description: Dives underwater the 1st turn, then attacks next turn.
hm08.effect: Teaches Rock Climb to a compatible Pokémon.

explorer-kit.name: Explorer Kit
explorer-kit.description: A bag filled with convenient tools for exploring. It provides access to the Underground.
explorer-kit.effect: Sends the trainer to the Underground. Only usable outside.

loot-sack.name: Loot Sack
loot-sack.description: A sturdy, spacious bag that is used to carry loot obtained in the coal mine.

rule-book.name: Rule Book
rule-book.description: It lists the rules for holding battles. For linked battles, you may choose which set of rules you wish to use.

poke-radar.name: Poké Radar
poke-radar.description: A tool that can search out Pokémon that are hiding in grass. Its battery is recharged as you walk.
poke-radar.effect: Designates several nearby patches of grass as containing Pokémon, some of which may be special radar-only Pokémon. Successive uses in a certain way create chains of encounters with the same species; longer chains increase the chance that a shiny Pokémon of that species will appear.

point-card.name: Point Card
point-card.description: A card that lists the Battle Points you have earned.

journal.name: Journal
journal.description: A notebook that keeps a day-to-day record of your adventure so far.
journal.effect: Records some of the trainer’s activities for the day.

seal-case.name: Seal Case
seal-case.description: A case for storing Seals that can be applied to the capsule cases of Poké Balls.
seal-case.effect: Contains Seals used for decorating Pokéballs.

fashion-case.name: Fashion Case
fashion-case.description: A fancy case for the tidy and organized storage of colorful Pokémon Accessories.

seal-bag.name: Seal Bag
seal-bag.description: A tiny bag that can hold ten Seals for decorating Poké Balls.

pal-pad.name: Pal Pad
pal-pad.description: A convenient notepad that is used for registering your friends and keeping a record of game play.
pal-pad.effect: Contains friend codes for up to 32 other players, as well as their sprite, gender, and basic statistics for those that have been seen on WFC.

works-key.name: Works Key
works-key.description: A large key for operating the doors of the Valley Windworks in the canyon. It was held by a Team Galactic Grunt.
works-key.effect: Opens the front door of the Valley Windworks. Reusable.

old-charm.name: Old Charm
old-charm.description: An ancient good-luck charm made of Pokémon bones to be taken to the elder of Celestic Town.
old-charm.effect: Given to Cynthia’s grandmother to get the Surf HM.

galactic-key.name: Galactic Key
galactic-key.description: A card key for disengaging security systems in the Galactic HQ. Losing it can result in punishment, apparently.
galactic-key.effect: Grants access to Galactic HQ in Veilstone City.

red-chain.name: Red Chain
red-chain.description: A mythical chain that is said to link the legendary Pokémon that created the Sinnoh region.

town-map.name: Town Map
town-map.description: Can be viewed anytime. Shows your present location.
town-map.effect: Displays a map of the region including the trainer’s position, location names, visited towns, gym locations, and where the trainer has been walking recently.

vs-seeker.name: Vs. Seeker
vs-seeker.description: A rechargeable unit that flags battle- ready TRAINERS.
vs-seeker.effect: Reveals trainers who want a rematch, by showing !! over their heads. Each use drains the battery; requires 100 steps to charge.

coin-case.name: Coin Case
coin-case.description: A case that holds up to 9,999 COINS.
coin-case.effect: Contains the Coins used by the Game Corner, to a maximum of 50,000.

old-rod.name: Old Rod
old-rod.description: Use by any body of water to fish for wild POKéMON.
old-rod.effect: Used to find Pokémon on the Old Rod list for an area, which are generally Magikarp or similar.

good-rod.name: Good Rod
good-rod.description: A decent fishing rod for catching wild POKéMON.
good-rod.effect: Used to find Pokémon on the Good Rod list for an area, which are generally mediocre.

super-rod.name: Super Rod
super-rod.description: The best fishing rod for catching wild POKéMON.
super-rod.effect: Used to find Pokémon on the Super Rod list for an area, which are generally the best available there.

sprayduck.name: Sprayduck
sprayduck.description: A watering can shaped like a PSYDUCK. It helps promote healthy growth of Berries planted in soft soil.

poffin-case.name: Poffin Case
poffin-case.description: A case for storing Poffin cooked from Berries.

bicycle.name: Bicycle
bicycle.description: A folding bicycle that is faster than the RUNNING SHOES.
bicycle.effect: Increases movement speed outside or in caves. In high gear, allows the trainer to hop over some rocks and ascend muddy slopes.

suite-key.name: Suite Key
suite-key.description: A key to one of the suites at the luxury hotel by a lake. For some odd reason, it often disappears.
suite-key.effect: Opens the locked building in the lakeside resort.

oaks-letter.name: Oak’s Letter
oaks-letter.description: A letter from Prof. Oak. Written in it is a request for you to go to Rt. 224.
oaks-letter.effect: Grants access to Flower Paradise and Shaymin.

lunar-wing.name: Lunar Wing
lunar-wing.description: A feather that glows like the moon. It is said to possess the power to dispel nightmares.
lunar-wing.effect: Cures the sailor’s son of his nightmares; no reward, only a side effect of seeing Cresselia.

member-card.name: Member Card
member-card.description: A card needed for entering the inn in Canalave City. Oddly, the last date marked on it was 50 years ago.
member-card.effect: Provides access to Newmoon Island and Darkrai.

azure-flute.name: Azure Flute
azure-flute.description: A flute that puts out echoing sounds that do not seem to be of this world. It is unknown who made it.
azure-flute.effect: Allows entry into the Hall of Origin. Unreleased.

ss-ticket.name: S.S. Ticket
ss-ticket.description: The ticket required for sailing on a ferry.
ss-ticket.effect: Allows passage on a ferry. The same item is used for different ferries between different games.

contest-pass.name: Contest Pass
contest-pass.description: The pass required for entering POKéMON CONTESTS.
contest-pass.effect: Allows the trainer to enter Contests.

magma-stone.name: Magma Stone
magma-stone.description: A stone formed when boulders melted in intensely hot magma, then hardened. Magma remains sealed inside.
magma-stone.effect: Causes Heatran to appear at Reversal Mountain. Unused prior to Black and White 2.

parcel.name: Parcel
parcel.description: A parcel entrusted into your care. You’re supposed to deliver it to your childhood friend who left Twinleaf Town.
parcel.effect: Given to the trainer’s rival in Jubilife City. Contains two Town Maps, one of which is given to the trainer upon delivery.

coupon-1.name: Coupon 1
coupon-1.description: A coupon to be exchanged for a Pokémon Watch (Pokétch for short). Three coupons are needed.
coupon-1.effect: One of three coupons needed to receive a Pokétch.

coupon-2.name: Coupon 2
coupon-2.description: A coupon to be exchanged for a Pokémon Watch (Pokétch for short). Three coupons are needed.
coupon-2.effect: One of three coupons needed to receive a Pokétch.

coupon-3.name: Coupon 3
coupon-3.description: A coupon to be exchanged for a Pokémon Watch (Pokétch for short). Three coupons are needed.
coupon-3.effect: One of three coupons needed to receive a Pokétch.

storage-key.name: Storage Key
storage-key.description: The key to the storage inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
storage-key.effect: Grants access to the Team Galactic warehouse in Veilstone City.

secret-potion.name: Secret Potion
secret-potion.description: A fantastic medicine dispensed by the pharmacy in Cianwood City. It fully heals a Pokémon of any ailment.
secret-potion.effect: Required to cure the Psyducks blocking Route 210 of their chronic headaches.

griseous-orb.name: Griseous Orb
griseous-orb.description: A glowing orb to be held by GIRATINA. It boosts the power of Dragon- and Ghost-type moves.
griseous-orb.effect: Held by giratina : Holder’s dragon and ghost moves have 1.2× their base power. Holder is in Origin Forme. This item cannot be held by any Pokémon but Giratina. When you enter the Union Room or connect to Wi-Fi, this item returns to your bag.

vs-recorder.name: Vs. Recorder
vs-recorder.description: An amazing device that can record a battle either between friends or at a special battle facility.
vs-recorder.effect: Optionally records wireless, Wi-Fi, and Battle Frontier battles. Tracks Battle Points earned in the Battle Frontier, and stores commemorative prints.

gracidea.name: Gracidea
gracidea.description: A flower sometimes bundled in bouquets to convey gratitude on special occasions like birthdays.
gracidea.effect: Used by trainer on a shaymin : Changes the target Shaymin from Land Forme to Sky Forme. This item cannot be used on a frozen Shaymin or at night. Sky Forme Shaymin will revert to Land Forme overnight, when frozen, and upon entering a link battle. This item must be used again to change it back.

secret-key.name: Secret Key
secret-key.description: The key to the CINNABAR ISLAND GYM’s entrance.
secret-key.effect: Used by trainer in the Galactic Eterna Building, on the ground floor, to the left of the TV : Unlocks the secret rotom room, in which there are five appliances which can change Rotom’s form.

apricorn-box.name: Apricorn Box
apricorn-box.description: A handy box where you can store up to 99 Apricorns of each kind.

berry-pots.name: Berry Pots
berry-pots.description: Handy containers for cultivating Berries wherever you go.
berry-pots.effect: Contains four portable pots of soil suitable for growing berries.

squirt-bottle.name: Squirt Bottle
squirt-bottle.description: A bottle used for watering plants in the Berry Pots.
squirt-bottle.effect: Required to water berries within the berry pots. Required to battle the sudowoodo on johto route 36. This item cannot be directly used from the bag.

lure-ball.name: Lure Ball
lure-ball.description: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon hooked by a Rod when fishing.
lure-ball.effect: Used by trainer in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost. If the wild Pokémon was encountered by fishing, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 3× normal.

level-ball.name: Level Ball
level-ball.description: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that are a lower level than your own.
level-ball.effect: Used by trainer in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost. If the trainer’s Pokémon’s level is higher than: * four times the wild Pokémon’s, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 8× normal. * than twice the wild Pokémon’s, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 4× normal. * the wild Pokémon’s, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 2× normal.

moon-ball.name: Moon Ball
moon-ball.description: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that evolve using the Moon Stone.
moon-ball.effect: Used by trainer in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost. If the wild Pokémon is a clefairy, nidoran m, nidoran f, jigglypuff, skitty, or any evolution thereof, the wild Pokémon has 4× its catch rate.

heavy-ball.name: Heavy Ball
heavy-ball.description: A Poké Ball for catching very heavy Pokémon.
heavy-ball.effect: Used by a trainer in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost. If the wild Pokémon weighs: * 409.6 kg (903.0 lb) or more, its catch rate is 40 more than normal. * 307.2 kg (677.3 lb) or more, its catch rate is 30 more than normal. * 204.8 kg (451.5 lb) or more, its catch rate is 20 more than normal. * less than 204.8 kg (451.5 lb), its catch rate is 20 less than normal.

fast-ball.name: Fast Ball
fast-ball.description: A Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch fast Pokémon.
fast-ball.effect: Used by a trainer in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost. : If the wild Pokémon’s base speed is 100 or more, its catch rate is 4× normal.

friend-ball.name: Friend Ball
friend-ball.description: A Poké Ball that makes caught Pokémon more friendly.
friend-ball.effect: Used by a trainer in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost. If caught, the wild Pokémon’s happiness starts at 200.

love-ball.name: Love Ball
love-ball.description: Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that are the opposite gender of your Pokémon.
love-ball.effect: Used by a trainer in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost. If the trainer’s Pokémon and wild Pokémon are of the same species but opposite genders, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 8× normal.

park-ball.name: Park Ball
park-ball.description: A special Poké Ball for the Pal Park.
park-ball.effect: Used by a trainer in battle : Catches a wild Pokémon. This item can only be used in pal park.

sport-ball.name: Sport Ball
sport-ball.description: A special Poké Ball for the Bug-Catching Contest.
sport-ball.effect: Used by a trainer in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. The wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 1.5× normal.

red-apricorn.name: Red Apricorn
red-apricorn.description: A red Apricorn. It assails your nostrils.
red-apricorn.effect: May be given to Kurt in azalea town to produce a level ball.

blue-apricorn.name: Blue Apricorn
blue-apricorn.description: A blue Apricorn. It smells a bit like grass.
blue-apricorn.effect: May be given to Kurt in azalea town to produce a lure ball.

yellow-apricorn.name: Yellow Apricorn
yellow-apricorn.description: A yellow Apricorn. It has an invigorating scent.
yellow-apricorn.effect: May be given to Kurt in azalea town to produce a moon ball.

green-apricorn.name: Green Apricorn
green-apricorn.description: A green Apricorn. It has a mysterious, aromatic scent.
green-apricorn.effect: May be given to Kurt in azalea town to produce a friend ball.

pink-apricorn.name: Pink Apricorn
pink-apricorn.description: A pink Apricorn. It has a nice, sweet scent.
pink-apricorn.effect: May be given to Kurt in azalea town to produce a love ball.

white-apricorn.name: White Apricorn
white-apricorn.description: A white Apricorn. It doesn’t smell like anything.
white-apricorn.effect: May be given to Kurt in azalea town to produce a fast ball.

black-apricorn.name: Black Apricorn
black-apricorn.description: A black Apricorn It has an indescribable scent.
black-apricorn.effect: May be given to Kurt in azalea town to produce a heavy ball.

dowsing-machine.name: Dowsing Machine
dowsing-machine.description: A device that signals an invisible item by sound.
dowsing-machine.effect: Used by trainer outside of battle : Searches for hidden items.

rage-candy-bar.name: Rage Candy Bar
rage-candy-bar.description: A famous candy in Mahogany Town. Many tourists like to buy them to take home.
rage-candy-bar.effect: May be traded for a tm64 in the vertical Underground Path.

red-orb.name: Red Orb
red-orb.description: A red, glowing orb said to contain an ancient power.
red-orb.effect: Causes groudon to appear in the embedded tower.

blue-orb.name: Blue Orb
blue-orb.description: A blue, glowing orb said to contain an ancient power.
blue-orb.effect: Causes kyogre to appear in the embedded tower.

jade-orb.name: Jade Orb
jade-orb.description: A shiny green orb that is said to have a legend and has a deep connection with the Hoenn region.
jade-orb.effect: Causes rayquaza to appear in the embedded tower.

enigma-stone.name: Enigma Stone
enigma-stone.description: A crystal ball that was excavated from the ground. A very beautiful stone that is covered with bits of rock and earth.
enigma-stone.effect: When taken to the pewter city museum, causes latias or latios to attack the trainer. The Pokémon to appear will be whicher can’t be encountered roaming in the wild.

unown-report.name: Unown Report
unown-report.description: A report of all the discovered kinds of UNOWN.
unown-report.effect: Lists which unown forms the trainer has caught.

blue-card.name: Blue Card
blue-card.description: A card to save points for the Buena’s Password show.
blue-card.effect: Allows the trainer to answer the daily question on Buena’s radio show. Records the points earned for correct answers.

slowpoke-tail.name: Slowpoke Tail
slowpoke-tail.description: A very tasty tail of something. It sells for a high price.

clear-bell.name: Clear Bell
clear-bell.description: A very old-fashioned bell that makes a gentle ringing.
clear-bell.effect: May be given to the Kimono Girls to summon ho oh to the top of the bell tower.

card-key.name: Card Key
card-key.description: A card-type door key used in SILPH CO’s office.
card-key.effect: Used by trainer outside of battle : Opens doors in the goldenrod city Radio Tower.

basement-key.name: Basement Key
basement-key.description: The key for NEW MAUVILLE beneath MAUVILLE CITY.
basement-key.effect: Used by trainer outside of battle : Opens the door to the basement tunnel under goldenrod city.

red-scale.name: Red Scale
red-scale.description: A scale from the red GYARADOS. It glows red like a flame.
red-scale.effect: May be traded to Mr. Pokémon for an exp share.

lost-item.name: Lost Item
lost-item.description: The Mime Jr. doll that was lost by the Copycat.
lost-item.effect: May be traded to the Copycat for a pass.

pass.name: Pass
pass.description: A ticket required for riding the Magnet Train. It allows you to ride whenever and however much you’d like.
pass.effect: Allows the trainer to ride the Magnet Train between goldenrod city and saffron city.

machine-part.name: Machine Part
machine-part.description: An important machine part for the Power Plant that was stolen.
machine-part.effect: Must be replaced in the power plant to power the Magnet Train.

silver-wing.name: Silver Wing
silver-wing.description: A strange, silvery feather that sparkles.
silver-wing.effect: Causes lugia to appear in the whirl islands.

rainbow-wing.name: Rainbow Wing
rainbow-wing.description: A mystical, rainbow feather that sparkles.
rainbow-wing.effect: Causes ho oh to appear at the top of bell tower.

mystery-egg.name: Mystery Egg
mystery-egg.description: A mysterious Egg obtained from Mr. Pokémon. What’s in the Egg is unknown.
mystery-egg.effect: Must be obtained to trigger the break-in at Professor Elm’s lab, the first rival battle, and access to johto route 31.

gb-sounds.name: GB Sounds
gb-sounds.description: A music player that allows you to listen to nostalgic songs. It’s operated with a single switch.
gb-sounds.effect: Used by trainer outside of battle : Changes the background music to the equivalent 8-bit music.

tidal-bell.name: Tidal Bell
tidal-bell.description: An old-fashioned bell with a gentle, soothing sound.
tidal-bell.effect: May be given to the Kimono Girls to summon lugia to the top of the bell tower.

data-card-01.name: Data Card 01
data-card-01.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many victories you’ve had.
data-card-01.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first place overall in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-02.name: Data Card 02
data-card-02.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many losses you’ve had.
data-card-02.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in last place overall in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-03.name: Data Card 03
data-card-03.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many times your Pokémon have dashed.
data-card-03.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have dashed in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-04.name: Data Card 04
data-card-04.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many times your Pokémon have jumped.
data-card-04.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have jumped in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-05.name: Data Card 05
data-card-05.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many times you’ve won the Hurdle Dash.
data-card-05.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Hurdle Dash.

data-card-06.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Relay Run.

data-card-07.name: Data Card 07

data-card-12.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have messed up in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-13.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have defeated themselves in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-14.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have tackled in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-15.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon have fallen in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-16.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Ring Drop.

data-card-17.name: Data Card 17

data-card-17.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Lamp Jump.

data-card-18.name: Data Card 18

data-card-18.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Circle Push.

data-card-19.name: Data Card 19
data-card-19.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many Link Pokéathlon wins you’ve had.
data-card-19.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first place overall in the Pokéathlon over wirelss.

data-card-20.name: Data Card 20
data-card-20.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many Link Pokéathlon losses you’ve had.
data-card-20.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in last place overall in the Pokéathlon over wireless.

data-card-21.name: Data Card 21
data-card-21.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many times you’ve won an event.
data-card-21.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first across all Pokéathlon events.

data-card-22.name: Data Card 22
data-card-22.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many times you’ve lost an event.
data-card-22.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in last across all Pokéathlon events.

data-card-23.name: Data Card 23
data-card-23.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many times you’ve switched Pokémon.
data-card-23.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has switched Pokémon in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-24.name: Data Card 24
data-card-24.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many times you’ve won Goal Roll.
data-card-24.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has come in first in the Pokéathlon Goal Roll.

data-card-25.name: Data Card 25
data-card-25.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many times your Pokémon won individual prizes.
data-card-25.effect: Records the number of times the trainer’s Pokémon received prizes in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-26.name: Data Card 26
data-card-26.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how many times you’ve instructed your Pokémon.
data-card-26.effect: Records the number of times the trainer has instructed Pokémon in the Pokéathlon.

data-card-27.name: Data Card 27
data-card-27.description: It holds Pokéathlon records. It’s a card that shows how much time you’ve spent in the Pokéathlon.
data-card-27.effect: Records the total time spent in the Pokéathlon.

lock-capsule.name: Lock Capsule
lock-capsule.description: A sturdy Capsule that can only be opened with a special key.

photo-album.name: Photo Album
photo-album.description: A nice photo album for storing all the photos taken along your adventure.

mach-bike.name: Mach Bike
mach-bike.description: A folding bicycle that more than doubles foot speed.
mach-bike.effect: Increases movement speed outside or in caves. Faster than the acro bike. Allows the trainer to ascend muddy slopes.

acro-bike.name: Acro Bike
acro-bike.description: A folding bicycle capable of jumps and wheelies.
acro-bike.effect: Increases movement speed outside or in caves. Slower than the mach bike. Can perform various tricks, allowing the trainer to reach certain special areas.

wailmer-pail.name: Wailmer Pail
wailmer-pail.description: A tool used for watering BERRIES and plants.

devon-goods.name: Devon Goods
devon-goods.description: A package that contains DEVON’s machine parts.
devon-goods.effect: Contains a machine part to be delivered to Captain Stern.

soot-sack.name: Soot Sack
soot-sack.description: A sack used to gather and hold volcanic ash.
soot-sack.effect: Collects soot when walking through tall grass on hoenn route 113.

pokeblock-case.name: Pokéblock Case
pokeblock-case.description: A case for holding POKéBLOCKS made with a BERRY BLENDER.

letter.name: Letter
letter.description: A letter to STEVEN from the PRESIDENT of the DEVON CORP.
letter.effect: Contains a letter to be delivered to Steven.

eon-ticket.name: Eon Ticket
eon-ticket.description: The ticket for a ferry to a distant southern island.
eon-ticket.effect: Provides access to southern island and either latias or latios, whichever is not available roaming around Hoenn.

scanner.name: Scanner
scanner.description: A device found inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
scanner.effect: May be traded to Captain Stern for a deep sea tooth or a deep sea scale.

go-goggles.name: Go-Goggles
go-goggles.description: Nifty goggles that protect eyes from desert sandstorms.
go-goggles.effect: Allows the trainer to enter the desert on hoenn route 111.

meteorite.name: Meteorite
meteorite.description: A meteorite found at METEOR FALLS.
meteorite.effect: RSE: May be traded to Professor Cozmo for tm27. FRLG: A meteorite to be delivered to Lostelle’s father.

rm-1-key.name: Rm. 1 Key
rm-1-key.description: A key that opens a door inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
rm-1-key.effect: Unlocks room 1 on the abandoned ship.

rm-2-key.name: Rm. 2 Key
rm-2-key.description: A key that opens a door inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
rm-2-key.effect: Unlocks room 2 on the abandoned ship.

rm-4-key.name: Rm. 4 Key
rm-4-key.description: A key that opens a door inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
rm-4-key.effect: Unlocks room 4 on the abandoned ship.

rm-6-key.name: Rm. 6 Key
rm-6-key.description: A key that opens a door inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
rm-6-key.effect: Unlocks room 6 on the abandoned ship.

devon-scope.name: Devon Scope
devon-scope.description: A device by DEVON that signals any unseeable POKéMON.
devon-scope.effect: Reveals invisble kecleon on the overworld.

oaks-parcel.name: Oak’s Parcel
oaks-parcel.description: A parcel for PROF. OAK from a POKéMON MART’s clerk.
oaks-parcel.effect: A parcel to be delivered to Professor Oak for a Pokédex.

poke-flute.name: Poké Flute
poke-flute.description: A sweet-sounding flute that awakens POKéMON.
poke-flute.effect: Wakes up sleeping Pokémon. Required to wake up sleeping snorlax on the overworld.

bike-voucher.name: Bike Voucher
bike-voucher.description: A voucher for obtaining a bicycle from the BIKE SHOP.
bike-voucher.effect: May be traded for a bicycle.

gold-teeth.name: Gold Teeth
gold-teeth.description: Gold dentures lost by the SAFARI ZONE’s WARDEN.
gold-teeth.effect: The Safari Zone warden’s teeth, to be returned to him for hm04.

lift-key.name: Lift Key
lift-key.description: An elevator key used in TEAM ROCKET’s HIDEOUT.
lift-key.effect: Operates the elevator in the Celadon Rocket Hideout.

silph-scope.name: Silph Scope
silph-scope.description: SILPH CO’s scope makes unseeable POKéMON visible.

fame-checker.name: Fame Checker
fame-checker.description: Stores information on famous people for instant recall.
fame-checker.effect: Records information on various famous people.

tm-case.name: TM Case
tm-case.description: A convenient case that holds TMs and HMs.

berry-pouch.name: Berry Pouch
berry-pouch.description: A convenient container that holds BERRIES.

teachy-tv.name: Teachy TV
teachy-tv.description: A TV set tuned to an advice program for TRAINERS.

tri-pass.name: Tri-Pass
tri-pass.description: A pass for ferries between ONE, TWO, and THREE ISLAND.
tri-pass.effect: Provides access to the first three Sevii Islands.

rainbow-pass.name: Rainbow Pass
rainbow-pass.description: For ferries serving VERMILION and the SEVII ISLANDS.
rainbow-pass.effect: Provides access to the Sevii Islands.

tea.name: Tea
tea.description: A thirst-quenching tea prepared by an old lady.
tea.effect: Used to bribe the saffron city guards for entry to the city.

mysticticket.name: MysticTicket
mysticticket.description: A ticket required to board the ship to NAVEL ROCK.
mysticticket.effect: Provides access to Navel Rock, ho oh, and lugia.

auroraticket.name: AuroraTicket
auroraticket.description: A ticket required to board the ship to BIRTH ISLAND.
auroraticket.effect: Provides access to Birth Island and deoxys.

powder-jar.name: Powder Jar
powder-jar.description: Stores BERRY POWDER made using a BERRY CRUSHER.
powder-jar.effect: Holds Berry Powder from Berry Crushing.

ruby.name: Ruby
ruby.description: An exquisite, red- glowing gem that symbolizes passion.
ruby.effect: Deliver to Celio for use in the Network Machine.

sapphire.name: Sapphire
sapphire.description: A brilliant blue gem that symbolizes honesty.
sapphire.effect: Deliver to Celio for use in the Network Machine.

magma-emblem.name: Magma Emblem
magma-emblem.description: A medal-like item in the same shape as TEAM MAGMA’s mark.
magma-emblem.effect: Provides access to the magma hideout in the jagged pass.

old-sea-map.name: Old Sea Map
old-sea-map.description: A faded sea chart that shows the way to a certain island.
old-sea-map.effect: Provides access to Faraway Island and mew.

douse-drive.name: Douse Drive
douse-drive.description: A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes Techno Blast to a Water-type move.
douse-drive.effect: Held by genesect : Holder’s buster is blue, and its techno blast is water-type.

shock-drive.name: Shock Drive
shock-drive.description: A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes Techno Blast to an Electric-type move.
shock-drive.effect: Held by genesect : Holder’s buster is yellow, and its techno blast is electric-type.

burn-drive.name: Burn Drive
burn-drive.description: A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes Techno Blast to a Fire-type move.
burn-drive.effect: Held by genesect : Holder’s buster is red, and its techno blast is fire-type.

sweet-heart.effect: Used on a friendly Pokémon : Restores 20 HP.

greet-mail.effect: Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading. Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon. Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC. Held : Holder cannot be placed in the PC. Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.

prism-scale.name: Prism Scale
prism-scale.description: A mysterious scale that evolves certain Pokémon. It shines in rainbow colors.
prism-scale.effect: Held by feebas : Holder evolves into milotic when traded.

eviolite.name: Eviolite
eviolite.description: A mysterious evolutionary lump. When held, it raises the Defense and Sp. Def of a Pokémon that can still evolve.
eviolite.effect: Held by a Pokémon that is not fully evolved : Holder has 1.5× Defense and Special Defense.

float-stone.name: Float Stone
float-stone.description: A very light stone. It reduces the weight of a Pokémon when held.

rocky-helmet.name: Rocky Helmet
rocky-helmet.description: If the holder of this item takes damage, the attacker will also be damaged upon contact.
rocky-helmet.effect: Held : When the holder is hit by a contact move, the attacking Pokémon takes 1/6 its max HP in damage.

air-balloon.name: Air Balloon
air-balloon.description: When held by a Pokémon, the Pokémon will float into the air. When the holder is attacked, this item will burst.
air-balloon.effect: Held : Holder is immune to ground-type moves, spikes, toxic spikes, and arena trap. This effect does not apply during gravity or ingrain. When the holder takes damage from a move, this item is consumed.

red-card.name: Red Card
red-card.description: A card with a mysterious power. When the holder is struck by a foe, the attacker is removed from battle.
red-card.effect: Held : When the holder takes damage directly from a move and does not faint, it switches out for another random, non-fainted Pokémon in its party. This effect does not activate if another effect prevents the holder from switching out.

ring-target.name: Ring Target
ring-target.description: Moves that would otherwise have no effect will land on the Pokémon that holds it.
ring-target.effect: Held : When one of the user’s types would render it immune to damage, that type is ignored for damage calculation.

binding-band.name: Binding Band
binding-band.description: A band that increases the power of binding moves when held.
binding-band.effect: Held : Moves used by the holder that trap and damage a target for multiple turns (e.g. bind, fire spin) inflict twice their usual per-turn damage.

absorb-bulb.name: Absorb Bulb
absorb-bulb.description: A consumable bulb. If the holder is hit by a Water-type move, its Sp. Atk will rise.
absorb-bulb.effect: Held : When the holder takes water-type damage from a move, its Special Attack rises by one stage and this item is consumed.

cell-battery.name: Cell Battery
cell-battery.description: A consumable battery. If the holder is hit by an Electric-type move, its Attack will rise.
cell-battery.effect: Held : When the holder takes electric-type damage from a move, its Attack rises by one stage and this item is consumed.

eject-button.name: Eject Button
eject-button.description: If the holder is hit by an attack, it will switch with another Pokémon in your party.
eject-button.effect: Held : When the holder takes damage directly from a move and does not faint, it switches out for another non-fainted Pokémon in its party, as chosen by the Trainer. This effect does not activate if another effect prevents the holder from switching out.

fire-gem.name: Fire Gem
fire-gem.description: A gem with an essence of fire. When held, it strengthens the power of a Fire-type move only once.
fire-gem.effect: Held : When the holder uses a damaging fire-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.

water-gem.description: A gem with an essence of water. When held, it strengthens the power of a Water-type move only once.

health-wing.name: Health Feather
health-wing.description: An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases the base HP of a single Pokémon.
health-wing.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases the target’s HP effort by 1.

muscle-wing.name: Muscle Feather
muscle-wing.description: An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases the base Attack stat of a single Pokémon.
muscle-wing.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases the target’s Attack effort by 1.

resist-wing.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases the target’s Defense effort by 1.

genius-wing.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases the target’s Special Attack effort by 1.

clever-wing.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases the target’s Special Defense effort by 1.

swift-wing.name: Swift Feather

swift-wing.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Increases the target’s Speed effort by 1.

pretty-wing.name: Pretty Feather
pretty-wing.description: Though this feather is beautiful, it’s just a regular feather and has no effect on Pokémon.

cover-fossil.name: Cover Fossil
cover-fossil.description: A fossil of an ancient Pokémon that lived in the sea in ancient times. It appears to be part of its back.
cover-fossil.effect: Give to a scientist in a museum to receive a tirtouga.

plume-fossil.name: Plume Fossil
plume-fossil.description: A fossil of an ancient Pokémon that flew in the sky in ancient times. It appears to be part of its wing.
plume-fossil.effect: Give to a scientist in a museum to receive an archen.

liberty-pass.name: Liberty Pass
liberty-pass.description: A special pass to go to Liberty Garden. Board the ship in Castelia City.
liberty-pass.effect: Allows passage on the castelia city ship, which leads to liberty garden and victini.

pass-orb.name: Pass Orb
pass-orb.description: A mysterious orb containing the power of the Unova region, to be used when generating Pass Power.
pass-orb.effect: Acts as currency to activate Pass Powers in the Entralink.

dream-ball.name: Dream Ball
dream-ball.description: A special Poké Ball that appears out of nowhere in a bag at the Entree Forest. It can catch any Pokémon.
dream-ball.effect: Can only be used in Entree Forest, to catch Pokémon encountered in the Dream World. Used in battle : Catches a wild Pokémon without fail.

poke-toy.name: Poké Toy
poke-toy.description: An item that attracts Pokémon. Use it to flee from any battle with a wild Pokémon.
poke-toy.effect: Used in battle : Ends a wild battle. Cannot be used in trainer battles.

prop-case.name: Prop Case
prop-case.description: A lovely case to store colorful Props for your Pokémon to wear in a musical.
prop-case.effect: Stores props for the Pokémon Musical.

dragon-skull.name: Dragon Skull
dragon-skull.description: A skull of a Pokémon which was said to have braved the angry waters to fly around the world.
dragon-skull.effect: Only used as a plot point; this item is given to the player and taken away in the same cutscene.

balm-mushroom.name: Balm Mushroom
balm-mushroom.description: A rare mushroom which gives off a nice fragrance. A maniac will buy it for a high price.

big-nugget.name: Big Nugget
big-nugget.description: A big nugget of pure gold that gives off a lustrous gleam. A maniac will buy it for a high price.

pearl-string.name: Pearl String
pearl-string.description: Very large pearls that sparkle in a pretty silver color. A maniac will buy them for a high price.

comet-shard.name: Comet Shard
comet-shard.description: A shard which fell to the ground when a comet approached. A maniac will buy it for a high price.

relic-copper.name: Relic Copper
relic-copper.description: A copper coin used in a civilization about 3,000 years ago. A maniac will buy it for a high price.

relic-statue.name: Relic Statue

relic-crown.name: Relic Crown

casteliacone.name: Casteliacone
casteliacone.description: Castelia City’s specialty, soft-serve ice cream. It heals all the status problems of a single Pokémon.
casteliacone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Cures any status ailment and confusion.

dire-hit-2.name: Dire Hit 2
dire-hit-2.description: It can be used many times to raise the critical-hit ratio of one Pokémon. It wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.
dire-hit-2.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Raises the target’s critical hit rate by two stages. This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.

x-speed-2.description: It sharply raises the Speed stat of a Pokémon in battle. It wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.

ability-urge.name: Ability Urge
ability-urge.description: When used, it activates the Ability of an ally Pokémon.
ability-urge.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Selects another friendly Pokémon at random. If that Pokémon’s ability is normally activated by some condition—i.e., is not continuous and passive—its effect is forcibly activated. This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.

item-drop.name: Item Drop
item-drop.description: When used, it causes an ally Pokémon to drop a held item.
item-drop.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Selects another friendly Pokémon at random. If that Pokémon is holding an item, that item is removed for the duration of the battle. This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.

item-urge.name: Item Urge
item-urge.description: When used, it causes an ally Pokémon to use its held item.
item-urge.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Selects another friendly Pokémon at random. If that Pokémon is holding an item normally activated by some condition—i.e., not continuous and passive—its effect is forcibly activated. This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.

reset-urge.name: Reset Urge
reset-urge.description: When used, it restores any stat changes of an ally Pokémon.
reset-urge.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Selects another friendly Pokémon at random. Removes all of that Pokémon’s stat changes. This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.

dire-hit-3.name: Dire Hit 3
dire-hit-3.description: It can be used many times to greatly raise a Pokémon’s critical-hit ratio. It wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.
dire-hit-3.effect: Used on a party Pokémon in battle : Raises the target’s critical hit rate by three stages. This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.

light-stone.name: Light Stone
light-stone.description: Reshiram’s body was destroyed and changed into this stone. It is said to be waiting for the emergence of a hero.
light-stone.effect: Summons reshiram for the final battle against N.

dark-stone.name: Dark Stone
dark-stone.description: Zekrom’s body was destroyed and changed into this stone. It is said to be waiting for the emergence of a hero.
dark-stone.effect: Summons zekrom for the final battle against N.

tm93.name: TM93
tm93.description: The user shrouds itself in electricity and smashes into its target. It also damages the user a little.
tm93.effect: Teaches wild charge to a compatible Pokémon.

tm94.name: TM94
tm94.description: The user attacks with a punch that can shatter a rock. It may also lower the target’s Defense stat.
tm94.effect: Teaches rock smash to a compatible Pokémon.

tm95.name: TM95
tm95.description: The user yells as if it is ranting about something, making the target’s Sp. Atk stat decrease.
tm95.effect: Teaches snarl to a compatible Pokémon.

xtransceiver.name: Xtransceiver
xtransceiver.description: A high-tech transceiver with a camera function. It allows up to four-way calls.
xtransceiver.effect: Makes four-way video calls. Used for plot advancement in-game, but also works with other players via the C-Gear.

gram-1.name: Gram 1
gram-1.description: An important letter which Wingull delivers.
gram-1.effect: Give to the wingull on unova route 13, along with gram 2 and gram 3, to receive tm89.

gram-2.name: Gram 2
gram-2.description: An important letter that Wingull delivers.
gram-2.effect: Give to the wingull on unova route 13, along with gram 1 and gram 3, to receive tm89.

gram-3.name: Gram 3
gram-3.description: An important letter which Wingull delivers.
gram-3.effect: Give to the wingull on unova route 13, along with gram 1 and gram 2, to receive tm89.

dragon-gem.name: Dragon Gem
dragon-gem.description: A gem with a draconic essence. When held, it strengthens the power of a Dragon-type move only once.
dragon-gem.effect: Held : When the holder uses a damaging dragon-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.

normal-gem.name: Normal Gem
normal-gem.description: A gem with an ordinary essence. When held, it strengthens the power of a Normal-type move only once.
normal-gem.effect: Held : When the holder uses a damaging normal-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.

medal-box.name: Medal Box
medal-box.description: A box-shaped machine that stores Medals and Medal information.
medal-box.effect: Holds medals recieved in the medal rally.

dna-splicers.name: DNA Splicers
dna-splicers.description: A splicer that fuses Kyurem and a certain Pokémon. They are said to have been one in the beginning.
dna-splicers.effect: Fuses Kyurem with Reshiram or Zekrom, or splits them apart again.

permit.name: Permit
permit.description: A permit needed to enter the Nature Preserve. Only a few know about this place.
permit.effect: Grants access to the Nature Preserve.

oval-charm.name: Oval Charm
oval-charm.description: An oval charm said to increase the chance of Eggs being found at the Day Care.
oval-charm.effect: Doubles the chance of two Pokémon producing an egg at the daycare every 255 steps.

shiny-charm.name: Shiny Charm
shiny-charm.description: A shiny charm said to increase the chance of finding a Shiny Pokémon.
shiny-charm.effect: Raises the chance of finding a shiny Pokémon.

plasma-card.name: Plasma Card
plasma-card.description: A card key needed to enter the password inside the Plasma Frigate.
plasma-card.effect: Required to progress in the Plasma Frigate.

grubby-hanky.name: Grubby Hanky
grubby-hanky.description: A handkerchief dropped by a regular at Café Warehouse. It smells faintly like a Pokémon.
grubby-hanky.effect: Appears in the Café Warehouse on Sunday; return to the customer with a Patrat on Thursday.

colress-machine.name: Colress Machine
colress-machine.description: A special device that wrings out the potential of Pokémon. It is an imperfect prototype.
colress-machine.effect: Wakes up the Crustle blocking the way in Seaside Cave.

dropped-item.name: Dropped Item
dropped-item.description: The Xtransceiver found at the Nimbasa City amusement park. It seems it belongs to a boy.
dropped-item.effect: Returned to Curtis or Yancy as part of a sidequest.

reveal-glass.name: Reveal Glass
reveal-glass.description: A glass that reveals the truth. It is a mysterious glass that returns a Pokémon back to its original shape.
reveal-glass.effect: Switches Tornadus, Thundurus, and Landorus between Incarnate and Therian Forme.

weakness-policy.name: Weakness Policy
weakness-policy.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. Attack and Sp. Atk sharply increase if the holder is hit with a move it’s weak to.
weakness-policy.effect: Held: When the holder is hit by a super effective move, its Attack and Special Attack raise by two stages.

assault-vest.name: Assault Vest
assault-vest.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This offensive vest raises Sp. Def but prevents the use of status moves.
assault-vest.effect: Raises the holder’s Special Defense to 1.5×. Prevents the holder from selecting a status move.

pixie-plate.name: Pixie Plate
pixie-plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Fairy-type moves.
pixie-plate.effect: Held: Fairy-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Fairy.

ability-capsule.name: Ability Capsule
ability-capsule.description: A capsule that allows a Pokémon with two Abilities to switch between these Abilities when it is used.
ability-capsule.effect: Switches a Pokémon between its two possible (non-Hidden) Abilities.

whipped-dream.name: Whipped Dream
whipped-dream.description: A soft and sweet treat made of fluffy, puffy, whipped and whirled cream. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.
whipped-dream.effect: Traded on a Swirlix: Holder evolves into Slurpuff.

sachet.name: Sachet
sachet.description: A sachet filled with fragrant perfumes that are just slightly too overwhelming. Yet it’s loved by a certain Pokémon.
sachet.effect: Traded on a Spritzee: Holder evolves into Aromatisse.

luminous-moss.name: Luminous Moss
luminous-moss.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Def if hit with a Water- type attack. It can only be used once.
luminous-moss.effect: Held: If the holder is hit by a damaging Water move, it consumes this item and raises its Special Defense by one stage.

snowball.name: Snowball
snowball.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Attack if hit with an Ice-type attack. It can only be used once.
snowball.effect: Held: If the holder is hit by a damaging Ice move, raises its Attack by one stage.

safety-goggles.name: Safety Goggles
safety-goggles.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. These goggles protect the holder from both weather-related damage and powder.
safety-goggles.effect: Held: Prevents damage from powder moves and the damage from Hail and Sandstorm.

rich-mulch.name: Rich Mulch
rich-mulch.description: Mulch to be used in a Berry field. It increases the Berry harvest without the need for particularly diligent care.
rich-mulch.effect: Increases the total number of Berries by 2.

surprise-mulch.name: Surprise Mulch
surprise-mulch.description: Mulch to be used in a Berry field. It causes strange, sudden mutations based on the combination of Berries.
surprise-mulch.effect: Increases the chance of Berry mutation.

boost-mulch.name: Boost Mulch
boost-mulch.description: Mulch to be used in a Berry field. It increases the Berry harvest that can be grown by diligent watering.
boost-mulch.effect: Causes soil to dry out in 4 hours.

amaze-mulch.name: Amaze Mulch
amaze-mulch.description: Mulch to be used in a Berry field. An amazing Mulch with the effects of Rich, Surprise, and Boost Mulch.
amaze-mulch.effect: Increases the total number of Berries by 2, increases the chance of Berry mutation, and causes soil to dry out in 4 hours.

gengarite.name: Gengarite
gengarite.description: One variety of the mysterious Mega Stones. Have Gengar hold it, and this stone will enable it to Mega Evolve during battle.
gengarite.effect: Held: Allows Gengar to Mega Evolve into Mega Gengar.

garchompite.description: One variety of the mysterious Mega Stones. Have Garchomp hold it, and this stone will enable it to Mega Evolve during battle.
garchompite.effect: Held: Allows Garchomp to Mega Evolve into Mega Garchomp.

roseli-berry.name: Roseli Berry
roseli-berry.description: If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the damage taken from one supereffective Fairy-type attack.
roseli-berry.effect: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Fairy-type attack to halve the damage.

kee-berry.name: Kee Berry
kee-berry.description: If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will increase the holder’s Defense if it’s hit with a physical move.
kee-berry.effect: Held: When the holder is hit by a physical move, increases its Defense by one stage.

maranga-berry.name: Maranga Berry
maranga-berry.description: If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will increase the holder’s Sp. Def if it’s hit with a special move.
maranga-berry.effect: Held: When the holder is hit by a special move, increases its Special Defense by one stage.

discount-coupon.name: Discount Coupon
discount-coupon.description: This special coupon allows you to buy items at a discount when you are shopping at a boutique.
discount-coupon.effect: Can be used at a boutique for a 50% discount. Consumed once used.

strange-souvenir.name: Strange Souvenir
strange-souvenir.description: An ornament depicting a Pokémon that is venerated as a protector in some region far from Kalos.

lumiose-galette.name: Lumiose Galette
lumiose-galette.description: A popular treat in Lumiose City. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.
lumiose-galette.effect: Cures all major status ailments and confusion.

jaw-fossil.name: Jaw Fossil
jaw-fossil.description: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived on the land. It looks as if it could be a piece of a large jaw.
jaw-fossil.effect: Can be revived into a Tyrunt.

sail-fossil.name: Sail Fossil
sail-fossil.description: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived on the land. It looks like the impression from a skin sail.
sail-fossil.effect: Can be revived into an Amaura.

fairy-gem.name: Fairy Gem
fairy-gem.description: A gem with an essence of the fey. When held, it strengthens the power of a Fairy-type move one time.
fairy-gem.effect: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Fairy move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.

adventure-rules.name: Adventure Guide
adventure-rules.description: This book contains all the points a new Trainer needs to know on a journey. It was handmade by a kind friend.

elevator-key.name: Elevator Key
elevator-key.description: A card key that activates the elevator in Lysandre Labs. It is emblazoned with Team Flare’s logo.
elevator-key.effect: Unlocks the elevator in Lysandre Labs.

holo-caster.name: Holo Caster
holo-caster.description: A device that allows users to receive and view hologram clips at any time. It is also used to chat with others.
holo-caster.effect: Displays cutscene conversations as the plot advances.

honor-of-kalos.name: Honor of Kalos
honor-of-kalos.description: A precious symbol that is awarded only to an individual who has done great things for the Kalos region.
honor-of-kalos.effect: Does nothing, but signifies becoming Champion.

intriguing-stone.name: Intriguing Stone
intriguing-stone.description: A rather curious stone that might appear to be valuable to some. It’s all in the eye of the beholder.
intriguing-stone.effect: Trade for a Sun Stone in X and Y, or Pidgeotite in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire.

lens-case.name: Lens Case
lens-case.description: A rather chic-looking case for carrying contact lenses.
lens-case.effect: Allows the player to change their eye color.

looker-ticket.name: Looker Ticket
looker-ticket.description: A ticket that was handmade by Looker. It’s decorated with a liberal amount of glittery paint.

mega-ring.name: Mega Ring
mega-ring.description: This ring contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying Mega Stones to Mega Evolve in battle.
mega-ring.effect: Allows the player’s Pokémon to Mega Evolve.

power-plant-pass.name: Power Plant Pass
power-plant-pass.description: This pass serves as an ID card for gaining access to the power plant that lies along Route 13.
power-plant-pass.effect: Permits entry to the Kalos Power Plant.

profs-letter.name: Prof’s Letter
profs-letter.description: A letter that Professor Sycamore wrote to your mother. A faint but pleasant perfume seems to cling to the paper.
profs-letter.effect: Traded to the player’s mom for the Town Map and a Potion.

roller-skates.name: Roller Skates
roller-skates.description: Attaches roller skates to the bottom of your shoes, allowing you to glide quickly around and perform tricks.
roller-skates.effect: Allows the player to move quickly and off the grid.

sprinklotad.name: Sprinklotad
sprinklotad.description: A watering can shaped like a Lotad. It helps promote the healthy growth of any Berries planted in good, soft soil.

tmv-pass.name: TMV Pass
tmv-pass.description: A commuter pass that allows the holder to ride the TMV between Lumiose City and Kiloude City at any time.

tm96.name: TM96
tm96.description: An attack that makes use of nature’s power. Its effects vary depending on the user’s environment.

tm97.name: TM97
tm97.description: The user releases a horrible aura imbued with dark thoughts. This may also make the target flinch.

tm98.name: TM98
tm98.description: Striking opponents over and over makes the user’s fists harder. Hitting a target raises the Attack stat.

tm99.name: TM99
tm99.description: The user damages opposing Pokémon by emitting a powerful flash.

tm100.name: TM100
tm100.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

latiasite.name: Latiasite
latiasite.description: One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones. Have Latias hold it, and this stone will enable it to Mega Evolve during battle.
latiasite.effect: Held: Allows Latias to Mega Evolve into Mega Latias.

latiosite.name: Latiosite
latiosite.description: One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones. Have Latios hold it, and this stone will enable it to Mega Evolve during battle.
latiosite.effect: Held: Allows Latios to Mega Evolve into Mega Latios.

common-stone.name: Common Stone
common-stone.description: A perfectly normal stone that might appear to be valuable to some. It’s all in the eye of the beholder.

makeup-bag.name: Makeup Bag
makeup-bag.description: A rather chic-looking case for carrying lipsticks.
makeup-bag.effect: Allows the player to change their lipstick color.

travel-trunk.name: Travel Trunk
travel-trunk.description: A light and yet incredibly effective trunk, which can contain every last piece of clothing that can be bought.
travel-trunk.effect: Unobtainable, but allows the player to change clothes anywhere.

shalour-sable.name: Shalour Sable
shalour-sable.description: Shalour City’s famous shortbread. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.
shalour-sable.effect: Cures any major status ailment and confusion.

mega-charm.name: Mega Charm
mega-charm.description: This charm contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying Mega Stones to Mega Evolve in battle.
mega-charm.effect: Unused. This appears as the girlplayer’s Mega Bracelet in Pokémon Contests, but it cannot actually be obtained.

mega-glove.name: Mega Glove
mega-glove.description: This glove contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying Mega Stones to Mega Evolve in battle.
mega-glove.effect: Unused. This is Korrina’s Key Stone in X and Y, but it cannot be obtained by the player.

devon-parts.name: Devon Parts
devon-parts.description: A case that contains mechanical parts of some sort made by the Devon Corporation.
devon-parts.effect: Allows Captain Stern to set out on his expedition.

pokeblock-kit.name: Pokéblock Kit
pokeblock-kit.description: A set containing a Berry Blender for making Pokéblocks and a Pokéblock Case for storing Pokéblocks.

key-to-room-1.name: Key to Room 1
key-to-room-1.description: A key that opens a door inside Sea Mauville.
key-to-room-1.effect: Unlocks the door to Room 1 in Sea Mauville.

key-to-room-2.name: Key to Room 2
key-to-room-2.description: A key that opens a door inside Sea Mauville.
key-to-room-2.effect: Unlocks the door to Room 2 in Sea Mauville.

key-to-room-4.name: Key to Room 4
key-to-room-4.description: A key that opens a door inside Sea Mauville.
key-to-room-4.effect: Unlocks the door to Room 4 in Sea Mauville.

key-to-room-6.name: Key to Room 6
key-to-room-6.description: A key that opens a door inside Sea Mauville.
key-to-room-6.effect: Unlocks the door to Room 6 in Sea Mauville.

devon-scuba-gear.name: Devon Scuba Gear
devon-scuba-gear.description: A device made by Devon Corporation that provides oxygen to users during the use of Dive.
devon-scuba-gear.effect: Worn by the player while underwater via Dive in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire.

contest-costume--jacket.name: Contest Costume
contest-costume--jacket.description: A very cool suit to be worn during the Contest Spectacular.

magma-suit.name: Magma Suit
magma-suit.description: A suit made with the collective technological know-how of Team Magma. It can withstand any impact.
magma-suit.effect: Allows the player to ride Groudon in the Cave of Origin.

aqua-suit.name: Aqua Suit
aqua-suit.description: A suit made with the collective technological know-how of Team Aqua. It can withstand any impact.
aqua-suit.effect: Allows the player to ride Kyogre in the Cave of Origin.

pair-of-tickets.name: Pair of Tickets
pair-of-tickets.description: Tickets for two to the astronomical show being held at the Mossdeep Space Center.
pair-of-tickets.effect: Allows the player and their mother to see the star show in the Mossdeep Space Center.

mega-bracelet.name: Mega Bracelet
mega-bracelet.description: This cuff contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to Mega Evolve in battle.
mega-bracelet.effect: Allows the player’s Pokémon to Mega Evolve.

mega-pendant.effect: Unused. This is Wally’s Key Stone in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, but it cannot be obtained by the player.

mega-glasses.effect: Unused. This is Maxie’s Key Stone in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, but it cannot be obtained by the player.

eon-flute.name: Eon Flute
eon-flute.description: A flute that can be used to summon Latios or Latias no matter where you are.

bottle-cap.name: Bottle Cap
bottle-cap.description: A beautiful bottle cap that gives off a silver gleam. Some people are happy to receive one.
bottle-cap.effect: Trade to Mr. Hyper to set one of a Pokémon’s genes to 31.

gold-bottle-cap.name: Gold Bottle Cap
gold-bottle-cap.description: A beautiful bottle cap that gives off a golden gleam. Some people are happy to receive one.
gold-bottle-cap.effect: Trade to Mr. Hyper to set all of a Pokémon’s genes to 31.

z-ring.name: Z-Ring
z-ring.description: A mysterious ring that enables Pokémon to use Z-Power. It requires both the willpower and the physical power of the Trainer wearing it.
z-ring.effect: Allows the player’s Pokémon to use Z-moves.

decidium-z--held.name: Decidium Z

decidium-z--held.effect: Held: Allows decidueye to upgrade spirit shackle into sinister arrow raid.

primarium-z--held.name: Primarium Z
primarium-z--held.description: This is a crystallized form of Z-Power. It upgrades Primarina’s Sparkling Aria to a Z-Move.
primarium-z--held.effect: Held: Allows primarina to upgrade sparkling aria into oceanic operetta.

tapunium-z--held.name: Tapunium Z
tapunium-z--held.description: This is a crystallized form of Z-Power. It upgrades the tapu’s Nature’s Madness to a Z-Move.
tapunium-z--held.effect: Held: Allows tapu koko, tapu lele, tapu bulu, and tapu fini to upgrade natures madness into guardian of alola.

marshadium-z--held.name: Marshadium Z
marshadium-z--held.description: This is a crystallized form of Z-Power. It upgrades Marshadow’s Spectral Thief to a Z-Move.
marshadium-z--held.effect: Held: Allows marshadow to upgrade spectral thief into soul stealing 7 star strike.

aloraichium-z--held.name: Aloraichium Z

aloraichium-z--held.effect: Held: Allows Alola raichu to upgrade thunderbolt into stoked sparksurfer.

snorlium-z--held.name: Snorlium Z
snorlium-z--held.description: This is a crystallized form of Z-Power. It upgrades Snorlax’s Giga Impact to a Z-Move.
snorlium-z--held.effect: Held: Allows snorlax to upgrade giga impact into pulverizing pancake.

eevium-z--held.effect: Held: Allows eevee to upgrade last resort into extreme evoboost.
