mewnium-z--held.description: This is a crystallized form of Z-Power. It upgrades Mew’s Psychic to a Z-Move.
mewnium-z--held.effect: Held: Allows mew to upgrade psychic into genesis supernova.

pikashunium-z--held.name: Pikashunium Z
pikashunium-z--held.description: This is a crystallized form of Z-Power. It upgrades a Thunderbolt by Pikachu in a cap to a Z-Move.
pikashunium-z--held.effect: Held: Allows cap-wearing pikachu to upgrade thunderbolt into 10 000 000 volt thunderbolt.

forage-bag.name: Forage Bag
forage-bag.description: A bag to carry ingredients gathered during Mallow’s trial in the jungle.

fishing-rod.name: Fishing Rod
fishing-rod.description: A Fishing Rod made by Captain Lana. Cast a line over piles of underwater rocks to fish for wild aquatic Pokémon.
fishing-rod.effect: Allows the player to fish for Pokémon.

professors-mask.name: Professor’s Mask
professors-mask.description: The mask that belongs to The Masked Royal. This pro wrestler apparently sews his mask on a machine himself.

festival-ticket.name: Festival Ticket
festival-ticket.description: A ticket that allows you to host a mission in Festival Plaza.
festival-ticket.effect: Hosts a mission in Festival Plaza.

sparkling-stone.name: Sparkling Stone
sparkling-stone.description: A stone entrusted by a Pokémon that has been venerated as a guardian deity in the Alola region. There is said to be some secret in how it sparkles.

adrenaline-orb.name: Adrenaline Orb
adrenaline-orb.description: Using it makes wild Pokémon more likely to call for help. If held by a Pokémon, it boosts Speed when intimidated. It can be used only once.
adrenaline-orb.effect: Makes wild Pokémon more likely to summon allies. Held: increases the holder’s Speed by one stage when affected by Intimidate.

zygarde-cube.name: Zygarde Cube
zygarde-cube.description: An item to store Zygarde Cores and Cells. You can also use it to teach Zygarde moves.
zygarde-cube.effect: Contains collected Zygarde cells/cores. Can teach Zygarde moves.

ice-stone.name: Ice Stone
ice-stone.description: A peculiar stone that can make certain species of Pokémon evolve. It has an unmistakable snowflake pattern.
ice-stone.effect: Used on a party Pokémon : Evolves an Alola sandshrew into Alola sandslash or an Alola vulpix into Alola ninetales.

ride-pager.name: Ride Pager
ride-pager.description: By entering certain numbers on this pager, you can summon Ride Pokémon in an instant.
ride-pager.effect: Allows the player to summon a Ride Pokémon. Unused, as this can be done simply by pressing Y.

beast-ball.name: Beast Ball
beast-ball.description: A special Poké Ball designed to catch Ultra Beasts. It has a low success rate for catching others.
beast-ball.effect: Used in battle : Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon. If the wild Pokémon is an Ultra Beast, this ball has a catch rate of 5×. Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 0.1×. If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

big-malasada.name: Big Malasada
big-malasada.description: The Alola region’s local specialty—fried bread. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.
big-malasada.effect: Cures major status ailments and confusion.

red-nectar.name: Red Nectar
red-nectar.description: A flower nectar obtained at Ula’ula Meadow. It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.
red-nectar.effect: Changes Oricorio to Baile Style. Single-use and cannot be used in battle.

yellow-nectar.name: Yellow Nectar
yellow-nectar.description: A flower nectar obtained at Melemele Meadow. It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.
yellow-nectar.effect: Changes Oricorio to Pom-Pom Style. Single-use and cannot be used in battle.

pink-nectar.name: Pink Nectar
pink-nectar.description: The flower nectar obtained at the flowering shrubs on Royal Avenue. It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.
pink-nectar.effect: Changes Oricorio to Pa’u Style. Single-use and cannot be used in battle.

purple-nectar.name: Purple Nectar
purple-nectar.description: A flower nectar obtained at Poni Meadow. It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.
purple-nectar.effect: Changes Oricorio to Sensu Style. Single-use and cannot be used in battle.

sun-flute.name: Sun Flute
sun-flute.description: It is said that the tones it produces were offered up as an expression of gratitude to the Legendary Pokémon of the sun.
sun-flute.effect: Evolves Nebby into Solgaleo when used at the Altar of the Sunne.

moon-flute.name: Moon Flute
moon-flute.description: It is said that the tones it produces were offered up as an expression of gratitude to the Legendary Pokémon of the moon.
moon-flute.effect: Evolves Nebby into Lunala when used at the Altar of the Moone.

enigmatic-card.name: Enigmatic Card
enigmatic-card.description: A mysterious card. Written on it is a request for you to go to a guest room in an Akala motel on Route 8.
enigmatic-card.effect: Unlocks Looker’s motel room on Route 8.

terrain-extender.name: Terrain Extender
terrain-extender.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the duration of the terrain caused by the holder’s move or Ability.
terrain-extender.effect: Held: When the holder changes the Terrain (whether by move or ability), it will last 8 turns instead of 5.

protective-pads.name: Protective Pads
protective-pads.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. These pads protect the holder from effects caused by making direct contact with the target.
protective-pads.effect: Held: Prevents side effects of contact moves used on the holder.

electric-seed.name: Electric Seed
electric-seed.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Defense on Electric Terrain. It can only be used once.
electric-seed.effect: Held: If the holder enters battle during Electric Terrain, or if Electric Terrain is activated while the holder is in battle, this item is consumed and the holder’s Defense raises by one stage.

psychic-seed.name: Psychic Seed
psychic-seed.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Def on Psychic Terrain. It can only be used once.
psychic-seed.effect: Held: If the holder enters battle during Psychic Terrain, or if Psychic Terrain is activated while the holder is in battle, this item is consumed and the holder’s Special Defense raises by one stage.

misty-seed.name: Misty Seed
misty-seed.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Def on Misty Terrain. It can only be used once.
misty-seed.effect: Held: If the holder enters battle during Misty Terrain, or if Misty Terrain is activated while the holder is in battle, this item is consumed and the holder’s Special Defense raises by one stage.

grassy-seed.effect: Held: If the holder enters battle during Grassy Terrain, or if Grassy Terrain is activated while the holder is in battle, this item is consumed and the holder’s Defense raises by one stage.

fighting-memory.description: A memory disc that contains Fighting-type data. It changes the type of the holder if held by a certain species of Pokémon.
fighting-memory.effect: Held: Changes Silvally to its Fighting form. Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Fighting.

basement-key--goldenrod.name: Basement Key
basement-key--goldenrod.description: A key that opens a door in the Goldenrod Tunnel.

xtransceiver--red.description: A high-tech transceiver with a camera function. It allows up to four-way calls.

xtransceiver--yellow.name: Xtransceiver
xtransceiver--yellow.description: A high-tech transceiver with a camera function. It allows up to four-way calls.

dna-splicers--merge.name: DNA Splicers
dna-splicers--merge.description: A splicer that fuses Kyurem and a certain Pokémon. They are said to have been one in the beginning.

dna-splicers--split.name: DNA Splicers
dna-splicers--split.description: A splicer that separates Kyurem and a certain Pokémon when they have been fused.

dropped-item--red.name: Dropped Item
dropped-item--red.description: The Xtransceiver found at the Nimbasa City amusement park. It seems it belongs to a boy.

dropped-item--yellow.name: Dropped Item
dropped-item--yellow.description: The Xtransceiver found at the Nimbasa City amusement park. It seems it belongs to a girl.

ss-ticket--hoenn.name: S.S. Ticket
ss-ticket--hoenn.description: Ticket required for sailing on a ferry.

contest-costume--dress.name: Contest Costume

meteorite--2.name: Meteorite
meteorite--2.description: A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It gives off a faint glow.

meteorite--3.name: Meteorite

meteorite--4.name: Meteorite
meteorite--4.description: A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. Some sort of pattern has risen to the surface, and it’s shining with seven colors of light.

lunalium-z--bag.name: Lunalium Z
lunalium-z--bag.description: It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade Lunala’s Moongeist Beam to an exclusive Z-Move.

ultranecrozium-z--bag.name: Ultranecrozium Z
ultranecrozium-z--bag.description: It’s a crystal that turns Necrozma fused with Solgaleo or Lunala into a new form.

mimikium-z--bag.description: It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade Mimikyu’s Play Rough to an exclusive Z-Move.

z-power-ring.name: Z-Power Ring
z-power-ring.description: A mysterious ring that enables Pokémon to use Z-Power. It requires both the willpower and the physical power of the Trainer wearing it.

pink-petal.name: Pink Petal
pink-petal.description: A pressed flower petal you receive from Mina during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven different kinds of petals.

orange-petal.name: Orange Petal
orange-petal.description: A pressed flower petal you receive from Ilima during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven different kinds of petals.

blue-petal.name: Blue Petal
blue-petal.description: A pressed flower petal you receive from Lana during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven different kinds of petals.

red-petal.name: Red Petal
red-petal.description: A pressed flower petal you receive from Kiawe during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven different kinds of petals.

green-petal.name: Green Petal
green-petal.description: A pressed flower petal you receive from Mallow during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven different kinds of petals.

yellow-petal.name: Yellow Petal
yellow-petal.description: A pressed flower petal you receive from Sophocles during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven different kinds of petals.

purple-petal.name: Purple Petal
purple-petal.description: A pressed flower petal you receive from Nanu during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven different kinds of petals.

rainbow-flower.name: Rainbow Flower
rainbow-flower.description: A flower made of the petals you received from captains. It’s proof that your growth has been recognized by them.

surge-badge.name: Surge Badge
surge-badge.description: A novelty badge that you can receive at the Kantonian Gym. Rumor has it that it imitates the Badge of a Gym somewhere.

n-solarizer--merge.name: N-Solarizer
n-solarizer--merge.description: A machine to fuse Necrozma, which needs light, and Solgaleo.

n-lunarizer--merge.name: N-Lunarizer
n-lunarizer--merge.description: A machine to fuse Necrozma, which needs light, and Lunala.

n-solarizer--split.name: N-Solarizer
n-solarizer--split.description: A machine to separate Necrozma, which needed light, from Solgaleo.

n-lunarizer--split.name: N-Lunarizer
n-lunarizer--split.description: A machine to separate Necrozma, which needed light, from Lunala.

ilimas-normalium-z.name: Ilima Normalium Z
ilimas-normalium-z.description: The Normalium Z you were entrusted with by Ilima. You were asked to place it on the pedestal at the back of Verdant Cavern.

left-poke-ball.name: Left Poké Ball
left-poke-ball.description: A Poké Ball with a Pokémon that lost its Trainer inside. It seems to be from Ula’ula Island.

roto-hatch.name: Roto Hatch
roto-hatch.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It helps Eggs hatch faster.

roto-bargain.name: Roto Bargain
roto-bargain.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It reduces the prices of products at Poké Marts by half.

roto-prize-money.name: Roto Prize Money
roto-prize-money.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It triples the prize money you receive after battle.

roto-exp-points.name: Roto Exp. Points
roto-exp-points.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It increases the Exp. Points your Pokémon receive after battle a little.

roto-friendship.name: Roto Friendship
roto-friendship.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It helps Pokémon in your party grow friendly faster.

roto-encounter.name: Roto Encounter
roto-encounter.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It increases the chance of encountering high-level wild Pokémon a lot for a certain period of time.

roto-stealth.name: Roto Stealth
roto-stealth.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It prevents you from encountering wild Pokémon for a certain period of time.

roto-hp-restore.name: Roto HP Restore
roto-hp-restore.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It fully restores the HP of your battling Pokémon.

roto-pp-restore.name: Roto PP Restore
roto-pp-restore.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It fully restores the PP of your battling Pokémon.

roto-boost.name: Roto Boost
roto-boost.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It raises all stats of your battling Pokémon.

roto-catch.name: Roto Catch
roto-catch.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It increases the chance to catch Pokémon a lot.

autograph.name: Autograph
autograph.description: The autograph you got from the Vermilion City Gym Leader, Lt. Surge. He wrote, “Good luck!”

clothing-trunk.name: Clothing Trunk
clothing-trunk.description: A very light trunk that can carry clothing for you and your Pokémon.

silver-razz-berry.name: Silver Razz Berry
silver-razz-berry.description: A Berry that makes it easier to catch Pokémon when given to them.

golden-razz-berry.name: Golden Razz Berry
golden-razz-berry.description: A Berry that makes it drastically easier to catch Pokémon when given to them.

silver-nanab-berry.name: Silver Nanab Berry
silver-nanab-berry.description: A Berry that calms wild Pokémon you’re trying to catch when given to them.

golden-nanab-berry.name: Golden Nanab Berry
golden-nanab-berry.description: A Berry that calms wild Pokémon you’re trying to catch drastically when given to them.

silver-pinap-berry.name: Silver Pinap Berry
silver-pinap-berry.description: A Berry that makes you more likely to get an item when given to Pokémon you’re trying to catch.

golden-pinap-berry.name: Golden Pinap Berry
golden-pinap-berry.description: A Berry that makes you drastically more likely to get an item when given to Pokémon you’re trying to catch.

secret-key--letsgo.name: Secret Key
secret-key--letsgo.description: The key that unlocks the front door of the Pokémon Gym on Cinnabar Island. It has a red decoration.

ss-ticket--letsgo.name: S.S. Ticket
ss-ticket--letsgo.description: The ticket required to board the S.S. Anne. It has a drawing of a ship on it.

parcel--letsgo.name: Parcel
parcel--letsgo.description: A parcel to be delivered to Professor Oak from Viridian City’s Poké Mart.

card-key--letsgo.name: Card Key
card-key--letsgo.description: A card key that unlocks doors in Silph Co.’s head office in Saffron City.

stretchy-spring.name: Stretchy Spring
stretchy-spring.description: A thin small spring. It’s totally stretched out, and what it’s used for is unknown.

chalky-stone.name: Chalky Stone
chalky-stone.description: A small whitish stone picked up at the edge of the road.

marble.name: Marble
marble.description: A round glass marble. You can see colored glass inside the transparent marble.

lone-earring.name: Lone Earring
lone-earring.description: A single earring that somebody dropped.

beach-glass.name: Beach Glass
beach-glass.description: A piece of colored glass. Waves have rounded its edges. It’s slightly rough to the touch.

gold-leaf.name: Gold Leaf
gold-leaf.description: A mysterious gold leaf. No trees with this leaf have been found yet.

silver-leaf.name: Silver Leaf
silver-leaf.description: A mysterious silver leaf. No trees with this leaf have been found yet.

polished-mud-ball.name: Polished Mud Ball
polished-mud-ball.description: A ball made of mud. It had been polished for such a long time that the surface is very shiny.

tropical-shell.name: Tropical Shell
tropical-shell.description: A beautiful white shell that may have drifted from a sea in a warm region.

leaf-letter--pikachu.name: Leaf Letter
leaf-letter--pikachu.description: A letter written on a leaf. Something is written using Pikachu’s footprints.

leaf-letter--eevee.name: Leaf Letter
leaf-letter--eevee.description: A letter written on a leaf. Something is written using Eevee’s footprints.

small-bouquet.name: Small Bouquet
small-bouquet.description: A small bouquet that was made with heart and soul for a single Trainer.

lure.name: Lure
lure.description: A glass bottle of perfume that makes rare Pokémon more likely to appear.

super-lure.name: Super Lure
super-lure.description: A glass bottle of perfume that makes rare Pokémon more likely to appear. It lasts longer than a Lure.

max-lure.name: Max Lure
max-lure.description: A glass bottle of perfume that makes rare Pokémon more likely to appear. It lasts longer than a Super Lure.

pewter-crunchies.name: Pewter Crunchies
pewter-crunchies.description: Pewter City’s famous crunchy snack. They can be used to heal all status conditions of a single Pokémon.

health-candy.name: Health Candy
health-candy.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When given to a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s HP.

mighty-candy.name: Mighty Candy
mighty-candy.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When given to a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Attack stat.

tough-candy.name: Tough Candy
tough-candy.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When given to a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Defense stat.

smart-candy.name: Smart Candy
smart-candy.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When given to a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Sp. Atk stat.

courage-candy.name: Courage Candy
courage-candy.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When given to a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Sp. Def stat.

quick-candy.name: Quick Candy
quick-candy.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When given to a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Speed stat.

health-candy-l.name: Health Candy L
health-candy-l.description: A big candy that is packed with energy. When given to a Pokémon at Lv. 30 or higher, it will increase that Pokémon’s HP.
